++           &amp;lt;label className="block text-sm font-medium"&amp;gt;Special Die&amp;lt;/label&amp;gt;
++           &amp;lt;select data-qa="special-die" value={String(special)} onChange={(e) =&amp;gt; setSpecial((isNaN(Number(e.target.value)) ? (e.target.value as SpecialDieFace) : (Number(e.target.value) as SpecialDieFace)))} className="w-full rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-3 py-2 text-sm text-neutral-900 shadow-sm focus:border-blue-500 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-300 dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100 dark:border-neutral-700"&amp;gt;

+  
+  export default function Play(): JSX.Element {
+-   const dispatch = useDispatch();
+-   const players = useSelector((s: RootState) =&amp;gt; s.players.players);
+-   const cardsState = useSelector((s: RootState) =&amp;gt; s.cards);
+-   const propsState = useSelector((s: RootState) =&amp;gt; s.properties);
+- 
+-   // //#local-state
+-   const [currentIndex, setCurrentIndex] = useState&amp;lt;number&amp;gt;(0);
+-   const [d6A, setD6A] = useState&amp;lt;number&amp;gt;(1);
+-   const [d6B, setD6B] = useState&amp;lt;number&amp;gt;(1);
+-   const [special, setSpecial] = useState&amp;lt;SpecialDieFace&amp;gt;(1);
+-   const [advice, setAdvice] = useState&amp;lt;string[]&amp;gt;([]);
+-   const [ownerId, setOwnerId] = useState&amp;lt;string&amp;gt;('');
+-   const [moneyDelta, setMoneyDelta] = useState&amp;lt;number&amp;gt;(0);
+-   const [moneyPlayerId, setMoneyPlayerId] = useState&amp;lt;string&amp;gt;('');
+- 
+-   // //#derived
+-   const lastChance = cardsState.decks.chance.discardPile[0];
+-   const lastCommunity = cardsState.decks.community.discardPile[0];
+-   const lastBus = cardsState.decks.bus.discardPile[0];
+- 
+-   const roll: DieRoll = useMemo(
+-     () =&amp;gt; ({
+-       d6A,
+-       d6B,
+-       special,
+-       isDouble: d6A === d6B,
+-       isTriple: d6A === d6B &amp;amp;&amp;amp; typeof special === 'number' &amp;amp;&amp;amp; special === d6A,
+-       isTripleOnes: d6A === 1 &amp;amp;&amp;amp; d6B === 1 &amp;amp;&amp;amp; special === 1,
+-     }),
+-     [d6A, d6B, special]
+-   );
+- 
+-   // //#handlers
+-   // WHY: Provide human guidance to GM based on roll and tile effects before committing actions.
+-   const onEvaluate = (): void =&amp;gt; {
+-     const result = evaluateRollAdvisories(roll, {
+-       currentIndex,
+-       isOwned: () =&amp;gt; false,
+-       isOwnedByOther: () =&amp;gt; false,
+-     });
+-     setAdvice(result.map((r) =&amp;gt; r.message));
+-   };
+- 
+-   // WHY: Move applies steps and emits events; GO payout (+200) occurs only when wrapping index.
+-   const onApplyMove = (): void =&amp;gt; {
+-     const moveSteps = d6A + d6B + (typeof special === 'number' ? special : 0);
+-     const toIndex = (currentIndex + moveSteps) % BOARD_TILES.length;
+-     const tile = getTileByIndex(toIndex);
+-     const ev: GameEvent = {
+-       id: crypto.randomUUID(),
+-       gameId: 'local',
+-       type: 'MOVE',
+-       payload: { from: currentIndex, to: toIndex, steps: moveSteps, message: &amp;#96;Moved to ${tile.name}&amp;#96; },
+-       createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
+-     };
+-     dispatch(appendEvent(ev));
+-     if (passedGo(currentIndex, toIndex)) {
+-       dispatch(
+-         appendEvent({
+-           id: crypto.randomUUID(),
+-           gameId: 'local',
+-           type: 'PASSED_GO',
+-           payload: { amount: 200, message: '+$200 for passing GO' },
+-           moneyDelta: +200,
+-           createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
+-         })
+-       );
+-     }
+-     setCurrentIndex(toIndex);
+-   };
+- 
+-   // WHY: Assign ownership of current tile to selected player (GM-driven purchase recording).
+-   const onBuyProperty = (): void =&amp;gt; {
+-     const tile = getTileByIndex(currentIndex);
+-     if (!(tile.type === 'property' || tile.type === 'railroad' || tile.type === 'utility')) return;
+-     if (!ownerId) return;
+-     dispatch(assignOwner({ tileId: tile.id, ownerId }));
+-     dispatch(
+-       appendEvent({
+-         id: crypto.randomUUID(),
+-         gameId: 'local',
+-         type: 'PURCHASE',
+-         payload: { tileId: tile.id, ownerId, message: &amp;#96;Purchased ${tile.name}&amp;#96; },
+-         createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
+-       })
+-     );
+-   };
+- 
+-   // WHY: Generic money adjust covers payouts/income; event carries signed delta for traceability.
+-   const onAdjustMoney = (): void =&amp;gt; {
+-     if (!moneyPlayerId || !Number.isFinite(moneyDelta) || moneyDelta === 0) return;
+-     dispatch(adjustPlayerMoney({ id: moneyPlayerId, delta: moneyDelta }));
+-     dispatch(
+-       appendEvent({
+-         id: crypto.randomUUID(),
+-         gameId: 'local',
+-         type: 'MONEY_ADJUST',
+-         payload: { playerId: moneyPlayerId, amount: moneyDelta, message: &amp;#96;${moneyDelta &amp;gt; 0 ? '+' : ''}${moneyDelta}&amp;#96; },
+-         moneyDelta,
+-         createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
+-       })
+-     );
+-     setMoneyDelta(0);
+-   };
+- 
+-   // WHY: Draw from deck and record for spectators; card resolution stays GM-driven in MVP.
+-   const onDrawCard = (deck: 'chance' | 'community' | 'bus'): void =&amp;gt; {
+-     dispatch(drawCard(deck));
+-     dispatch(
+-       appendEvent({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), gameId: 'local', type: 'CARD', payload: { deck, message: &amp;#96;Drew from ${deck}&amp;#96; }, createdAt: new Date().toISOString() })
+-     );
+-   };
+- 
+-   // WHY: Compute rent using ownership/improvements/monopoly; transfer from selected player to tile owner.
+-   const onPayRent = (): void =&amp;gt; {
+-     const tile = getTileByIndex(currentIndex);
+-     const state = (window as any).__store__?.getState?.() as RootState | undefined;
+-     const diceTotal = d6A + d6B + (typeof special === 'number' ? special : 0);
+-     const rent = computeRent({ ...(state as any), properties: propsState } as RootState, tile.id, diceTotal);
+-     const ownerIdForTile = propsState.byTileId[tile.id]?.ownerId as string | null;
+-     if (!moneyPlayerId || !ownerIdForTile || rent &amp;lt;= 0) return;
+-     dispatch(adjustPlayerMoney({ id: moneyPlayerId, delta: -rent }));
+-     dispatch(adjustPlayerMoney({ id: ownerIdForTile, delta: +rent }));
+-     dispatch(
+-       appendEvent({
+-         id: crypto.randomUUID(),
+-         gameId: 'local',
+-         type: 'RENT',
+-         payload: { tileId: tile.id, from: moneyPlayerId, to: ownerIdForTile, amount: rent, message: &amp;#96;Rent ${rent}&amp;#96; },
+-         moneyDelta: -rent,
+-         createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
+-       })
+-     );
+-   };
+- 
+-   // WHY: Flat tax recorded as fee; GM picks the paying player.
+-   const onPayTax = (): void =&amp;gt; {
+-     const tile = getTileByIndex(currentIndex);
+-     if (tile.type !== 'tax' || !tile.taxAmount || !moneyPlayerId) return;
+-     dispatch(adjustPlayerMoney({ id: moneyPlayerId, delta: -tile.taxAmount }));
+-     dispatch(
+-       appendEvent({
+-         id: crypto.randomUUID(),
+-         gameId: 'local',
+-         type: 'FEE',
+-         payload: { tileId: tile.id, from: moneyPlayerId, amount: tile.taxAmount, message: &amp;#96;Tax ${tile.taxAmount}&amp;#96; },
+-         moneyDelta: -tile.taxAmount,
+-         createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
+-       })
+-     );
+-   };
+- 
+-   // WHY: Mortgage status toggles; validation lives in reducer (e.g., cannot mortgage with improvements).
+-   const onMortgageToggle = (mortgaged: boolean): void =&amp;gt; {
+-     const tile = getTileByIndex(currentIndex);
+-     if (!(tile.type === 'property' || tile.type === 'railroad' || tile.type === 'utility')) return;
+-     dispatch(setMortgaged({ tileId: tile.id, mortgaged }));
+-     dispatch(appendEvent({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), gameId: 'local', type: 'MONEY_ADJUST', payload: { tileId: tile.id, mortgaged }, createdAt: new Date().toISOString() }));
+-   };
+- 
+-   // WHY: Even-building enforced in reducer; hotel swap updates bank inventory accordingly.
+-   const onBuild = (): void =&amp;gt; {
+-     const tile = getTileByIndex(currentIndex);
+-     if (tile.type !== 'property') return;
+-     dispatch(buyHouse({ tileId: tile.id, ownerId: ownerId || (propsState.byTileId[tile.id]?.ownerId as string) }));
+-     dispatch(appendEvent({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), gameId: 'local', type: 'MONEY_ADJUST', payload: { tileId: tile.id, message: 'Build' }, createdAt: new Date().toISOString() }));
+-   };
+- 
+-   const onSell = (): void =&amp;gt; {
+-     const tile = getTileByIndex(currentIndex);
+-     if (tile.type !== 'property') return;
+-     dispatch(sellHouse({ tileId: tile.id }));
+-     dispatch(appendEvent({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), gameId: 'local', type: 'MONEY_ADJUST', payload: { tileId: tile.id, message: 'Sell' }, createdAt: new Date().toISOString() }));
+-   };
+- 
+-   // //#render
+    return (
+      &amp;lt;div data-qa="play-page" className="min-h-dvh flex items-center justify-center p-6 bg-neutral-50 text-neutral-900 dark:bg-neutral-950 dark:text-neutral-50"&amp;gt;
+-       &amp;lt;div className="w-full max-w-3xl space-y-6"&amp;gt;
+-         &amp;lt;h1 className="text-2xl font-semibold"&amp;gt;GM Console&amp;lt;/h1&amp;gt;
+- 
+-         {/* //#gm-inputs: dice/current index inputs */}
+-         &amp;lt;div data-qa="gm-inputs" className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-4"&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;div className="space-y-2"&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;label className="block text-sm font-medium"&amp;gt;Current Tile Index&amp;lt;/label&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;input data-qa="current-index" type="number" value={currentIndex} onChange={(e) =&amp;gt; setCurrentIndex(parseInt(e.target.value || '0', 10))} className="w-full rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-3 py-2 text-sm text-neutral-900 shadow-sm focus:border-blue-500 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-300 dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100 dark:border-neutral-700" /&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;div className="space-y-2"&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;label className="block text-sm font-medium"&amp;gt;D6 A&amp;lt;/label&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;input data-qa="d6a" type="number" min={1} max={6} value={d6A} onChange={(e) =&amp;gt; setD6A(Math.max(1, Math.min(6, parseInt(e.target.value || '1', 10))))} className="w-full rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-3 py-2 text-sm text-neutral-900 shadow-sm focus:border-blue-500 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-300 dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100 dark:border-neutral-700" /&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;div className="space-y-2"&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;label className="block text-sm font-medium"&amp;gt;D6 B&amp;lt;/label&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;input data-qa="d6b" type="number" min={1} max={6} value={d6B} onChange={(e) =&amp;gt; setD6B(Math.max(1, Math.min(6, parseInt(e.target.value || '1', 10))))} className="w-full rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-3 py-2 text-sm text-neutral-900 shadow-sm focus:border-blue-500 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-300 dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100 dark:border-neutral-700" /&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;div className="space-y-2 md:col-span-3"&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;label className="block text-sm font-medium"&amp;gt;Special Die&amp;lt;/label&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;select data-qa="special-die" value={String(special)} onChange={(e) =&amp;gt; setSpecial((isNaN(Number(e.target.value)) ? (e.target.value as SpecialDieFace) : (Number(e.target.value) as SpecialDieFace)))} className="w-full rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-3 py-2 text-sm text-neutral-900 shadow-sm focus:border-blue-500 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-300 dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100 dark:border-neutral-700"&amp;gt;
+-               &amp;lt;option value="1"&amp;gt;1&amp;lt;/option&amp;gt;
+-               &amp;lt;option value="2"&amp;gt;2&amp;lt;/option&amp;gt;
+-               &amp;lt;option value="3"&amp;gt;3&amp;lt;/option&amp;gt;
+-               &amp;lt;option value="+1"&amp;gt;+1&amp;lt;/option&amp;gt;
+-               &amp;lt;option value="-1"&amp;gt;-1&amp;lt;/option&amp;gt;
+-               &amp;lt;option value="Bus"&amp;gt;Bus&amp;lt;/option&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;/select&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-         &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+- 
+-         {/* //#gm-actions: record board interactions and finances */}
+-         &amp;lt;div data-qa="gm-actions" className="flex flex-wrap gap-3"&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;button data-qa="btn-evaluate" onClick={onEvaluate} className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 bg-blue-600 text-white font-semibold shadow hover:bg-blue-700 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-400"&amp;gt;Evaluate Roll&amp;lt;/button&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;button data-qa="btn-apply-move" onClick={onApplyMove} className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 bg-emerald-600 text-white font-semibold shadow hover:bg-emerald-700 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-emerald-400"&amp;gt;Apply Move&amp;lt;/button&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;div className="inline-flex items-center gap-2"&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;select data-qa="owner-picker" value={ownerId} onChange={(e) =&amp;gt; setOwnerId(e.target.value)} className="rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-3 py-2 text-sm text-neutral-900 shadow-sm focus:border-blue-500 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-300 dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100 dark:border-neutral-700"&amp;gt;
+-               &amp;lt;option value=""&amp;gt;Select owner&amp;lt;/option&amp;gt;
+-               {players.map((p) =&amp;gt; (
+-                 &amp;lt;option value={p.id} key={p.id}&amp;gt;
+-                   {p.nickname}
+-                 &amp;lt;/option&amp;gt;
+-               ))}
+-             &amp;lt;/select&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;button data-qa="btn-buy-property" onClick={onBuyProperty} className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 bg-amber-600 text-white font-semibold shadow hover:bg-amber-700 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-amber-400"&amp;gt;Buy Property&amp;lt;/button&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;div className="inline-flex items-center gap-2"&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;select data-qa="money-player" value={moneyPlayerId} onChange={(e) =&amp;gt; setMoneyPlayerId(e.target.value)} className="rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-3 py-2 text-sm text-neutral-900 shadow-sm focus:border-blue-500 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-300 dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100 dark:border-neutral-700"&amp;gt;
+-               &amp;lt;option value=""&amp;gt;Select player&amp;lt;/option&amp;gt;
+-               {players.map((p) =&amp;gt; (
+-                 &amp;lt;option value={p.id} key={p.id}&amp;gt;
+-                   {p.nickname}
+-                 &amp;lt;/option&amp;gt;
+-               ))}
+-             &amp;lt;/select&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;input data-qa="money-delta" type="number" value={moneyDelta} onChange={(e) =&amp;gt; setMoneyDelta(parseInt(e.target.value || '0', 10))} className="w-28 rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-3 py-2 text-sm text-neutral-900 shadow-sm focus:border-blue-500 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-300 dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100 dark:border-neutral-700" /&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;button data-qa="btn-apply-money" onClick={onAdjustMoney} className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-3 py-2 bg-slate-700 text-white font-semibold shadow hover:bg-slate-800 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-slate-400"&amp;gt;Apply Money&amp;lt;/button&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;button data-qa="btn-pay-rent" onClick={onPayRent} className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 bg-rose-600 text-white font-semibold shadow hover:bg-rose-700 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-rose-400"&amp;gt;Pay Rent&amp;lt;/button&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;button data-qa="btn-pay-tax" onClick={onPayTax} className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 bg-orange-600 text-white font-semibold shadow hover:bg-orange-700 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-orange-400"&amp;gt;Pay Tax&amp;lt;/button&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;button data-qa="btn-mortgage" onClick={() =&amp;gt; onMortgageToggle(true)} className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 bg-zinc-600 text-white font-semibold shadow hover:bg-zinc-700 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-zinc-400"&amp;gt;Mortgage&amp;lt;/button&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;button data-qa="btn-unmortgage" onClick={() =&amp;gt; onMortgageToggle(false)} className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 bg-zinc-500 text-white font-semibold shadow hover:bg-zinc-600 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-zinc-300"&amp;gt;Unmortgage&amp;lt;/button&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;button data-qa="btn-build" onClick={onBuild} className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 bg-green-700 text-white font-semibold shadow hover:bg-green-800 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-green-500"&amp;gt;Build&amp;lt;/button&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;button data-qa="btn-sell" onClick={onSell} className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 bg-yellow-700 text-white font-semibold shadow hover:bg-yellow-800 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-yellow-500"&amp;gt;Sell&amp;lt;/button&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;button data-qa="btn-draw-chance" onClick={() =&amp;gt; onDrawCard('chance')} className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 bg-purple-600 text-white font-semibold shadow hover:bg-purple-700 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-purple-400"&amp;gt;Draw Chance&amp;lt;/button&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;button data-qa="btn-draw-community" onClick={() =&amp;gt; onDrawCard('community')} className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 bg-fuchsia-600 text-white font-semibold shadow hover:bg-fuchsia-700 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-fuchsia-400"&amp;gt;Draw Community&amp;lt;/button&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;button data-qa="btn-draw-bus" onClick={() =&amp;gt; onDrawCard('bus')} className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-4 py-2 bg-cyan-600 text-white font-semibold shadow hover:bg-cyan-700 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-cyan-400"&amp;gt;Draw Bus&amp;lt;/button&amp;gt;
+-         &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+- 
+-         {/* //#cards-last: quick reference of last drawn cards for GM */}
+-         &amp;lt;div data-qa="cards-last" className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-4"&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;div className="rounded-lg border border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-700 p-3 text-sm"&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;div className="font-semibold mb-1"&amp;gt;Last Chance&amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;div className="opacity-80 min-h-10"&amp;gt;{lastChance ? lastChance.text : '—'}&amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;div className="rounded-lg border border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-700 p-3 text-sm"&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;div className="font-semibold mb-1"&amp;gt;Last Community Chest&amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;div className="opacity-80 min-h-10"&amp;gt;{lastCommunity ? lastCommunity.text : '—'}&amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;div className="rounded-lg border border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-700 p-3 text-sm"&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;div className="font-semibold mb-1"&amp;gt;Last Bus&amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-             &amp;lt;div className="opacity-80 min-h-10"&amp;gt;{lastBus ? lastBus.text : '—'}&amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+-         &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+- 
+-         {/* //#advisories: human-readable suggestions before committing actions */}
+-         &amp;lt;div data-qa="advisories" className="space-y-2"&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;h2 className="text-lg font-medium"&amp;gt;Advisories&amp;lt;/h2&amp;gt;
+-           &amp;lt;ul className="list-disc pl-5 space-y-1 text-sm opacity-90"&amp;gt;
+-             {advice.map((msg, i) =&amp;gt; (
+-               &amp;lt;li key={i}&amp;gt;{msg}&amp;lt;/li&amp;gt;
+-             ))}
+-           &amp;lt;/ul&amp;gt;
+-         &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+- 
+-         {/* //#event-log: chronological record of GM actions for spectators and undo later */}
+-         &amp;lt;EventLog /&amp;gt;
+-       &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
++       &amp;lt;PlayConsole /&amp;gt;
+      &amp;lt;/div&amp;gt;
+    );

++ // #index
++ // - //#imports: hooks and selectors for spectator data
++ // - //#render: spectator dashboard (players, bank, unowned, most landed, groups)
++ 
++ // //#imports
++ import React from 'react';
++ import { useSelector } from 'react-redux';
++ import { selectSpectatorSummary, selectBankBuildingCounts, selectPlayerPropertyDetails, selectUnownedProperties, selectColorGroupOwnership, selectMostLandedTile } from '@/features/selectors/stats';
++ 
++ export default function SpectatorDashboard(): JSX.Element {
++   const players = useSelector(selectSpectatorSummary);
++   const playerDetails = useSelector(selectPlayerPropertyDetails);
++   const bank = useSelector(selectBankBuildingCounts);
++   const unowned = useSelector(selectUnownedProperties);
++   const groupOwnership = useSelector(selectColorGroupOwnership);
++   const mostLanded = useSelector(selectMostLandedTile);
++ 
++   // //#render
++   return (
++     &amp;lt;div className="w-full max-w-6xl space-y-6"&amp;gt;
++       {/* //#spectate-players: money and owned properties preview per player */}
++       &amp;lt;section data-qa="spectate-players" className="space-y-3"&amp;gt;
++         &amp;lt;h2 className="text-lg font-medium"&amp;gt;Players&amp;lt;/h2&amp;gt;
++         &amp;lt;div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-4"&amp;gt;
++           {players.map((p) =&amp;gt; {
++             const detail = playerDetails.find((d) =&amp;gt; d.playerId === p.id);
++             return (
++               &amp;lt;div key={p.id} className="rounded-lg border border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-700 p-4"&amp;gt;
++                 &amp;lt;div className="flex items-center justify-between"&amp;gt;

+_**User (2025-08-12 01:55Z)**_
+
+what is the "card shuffle Seed" input for?
+
+---
+
+_**Agent (model gpt-5, mode Agent)**_
+
+&lt;think&gt;&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Thought Process&lt;/summary&gt;
+**Explaining card shuffle seed input**
+
+I’m focusing on why the card shuffle seed input is important. It provides a deterministic shuffle for Chance, Community, and Bus deck shuffling using seededShuffle, which means the same seed generates the same card sequence across devices. In our GM-driven MVP, it initializes the deck state and enables reshuffling after exhaustion. Entering the same text or number yields the same order. If we change the seed mid-game, it won't affect the current draw pile unless setSeed is called again. This ensures a fair testing environment!
+**Clarifying seed input purpose**
+
+The user wants to know what the seed input is for. I should keep my answer minimal yet helpful. While our instructions mention providing a status update and summary, I think in this case, simply answering the question suffices. The seed input is essential for generating deterministic sequences in card shuffling, helping maintain fairness across game sessions. So, I’ll provide a brief explanation focusing on its purpose and importance in the context of gameplay.&lt;/details&gt;&lt;/think&gt;
+
+---
+
+- It sets the deterministic seed used to shuffle the Chance, Community Chest, and Bus decks. Same seed → same card order every time.
+- You can type any text or number. On “Start Game” we initialize the decks with that seed.
+- When a deck empties, it reshuffles deterministically using the base seed plus an increment so reshuffles are reproducible too.
+
+---
+

+_**User (2025-08-12 01:55Z)**_
+
+if i make changes to the code, will the local browser refresh and show changes?
+
+---
+
+_**Agent (model gpt-5, mode Agent)**_
+
+&lt;think&gt;&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Thought Process&lt;/summary&gt;
+**Clarifying Vite Dev Server and HMR**
+
+Yes, the Vite dev server does support Hot Module Replacement (HMR), which means it automatically refreshes or hot-reloads. You can run your project using &#96;npm run dev&#96;, and it's available at &#96;localhost:5173&#96;. 
+
+Edits made in the &#96;src&#96; folder will reflect live, but changes to configs like Tailwind classes or &#96;tsconfig&#96; may require a restart. When editing Redux slices, HMR typically preserves the state, but a full reload might be needed to see new initializations.&lt;/details&gt;&lt;/think&gt;
+
+---
+
+Yes. With the Vite dev server running at http://localhost:5173, edits in src/* hot-reload automatically (HMR). Notes:
+- UI/components/styles (Tailwind classes): instant update.
+- Redux slices/selectors: usually HMR; sometimes hard refresh to reset state.
+- Config changes (vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json), dependency installs, or Tailwind upgrade: restart dev server.
+
+---
+

+
+---
+
+- It deterministically shuffles the Chance, Community Chest, and Bus decks. Same seed → same card order every time, including reshuffles.
+- Why it’s useful:
+  - Replays/debugging: you can reproduce a game exactly.
+  - Fairness/verification: spectators and devices see the same deck order.
+  - Testing: stable results for automated checks.
+- How it works here: we store your seed and build decks with a seeded PRNG; when a deck empties, we reshuffle using the base seed plus a step so reshuffles are reproducible too.
+
+---
+

++     setOrder(players);
++   }, [players]);
++ 
++   const handleReorder = (newOrder: TurnPlayer[]) =&amp;gt; {

++     onReorder(newOrder.map((p) =&amp;gt; p.id));

++           &amp;lt;/Reorder.Item&amp;gt;
++         ))}
++       &amp;lt;/Reorder.Group&amp;gt;

+- After Start Game, should the toggle be locked (no opt-in/out), yes?

+
+Added Race pot (🏃‍➡️💰) opt-in per player in the combined list:
+- Each row now has a toggle with tooltip: “Race pot. cost:$100. First to go wins the pot.”
+- Toggling sets &#96;racePotOptIn&#96; in state; players are locked at Start Game.

+&lt;think&gt;&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Thought Process&lt;/summary&gt;
+**Troubleshooting Toggle Changes**
+
+I need to figure out why the user isn't seeing the toggle changes when setting up the game. The earlier implementation had an input checkbox inside a label, but now in the current repo, &#96;Toggle.tsx&#96; is set as a button with a role of switch. It looks like &#96;SetupForm&#96; might not include &#96;racePotOptIn&#96;, and earlier it used a raw action for changes. I believe I’ve fixed it, but I still need to verify the UI shows the updated toggle style.
+**Analyzing Toggle Visibility Issues**
+
+The &#96;Toggle.tsx&#96; in the repo does use the correct colors and knob movements, which matches what I expect. However, the user isn't seeing these changes, so I need to explore potential causes. 
+
+First, the dev server might not be running, or the page could be cached. They should try running the dev server again or rebuilding it. 
+
+Also, I realized the mapping of &#96;racePotOptIn&#96; wasn't included previously, which could mean toggles wouldn’t behave as expected. Finally, it's worth checking if the &#96;qa&#96; prop changed, as it might be looking for &#96;qa?: string&#96; instead of an id.
+**Reviewing Toggle Implementation**
+
+In the &#96;TurnOrderList&#96;, I'm passing &#96;qa={&#96;toggle-race-${p.id}&#96;}&#96;, which seems fine. The click on the emoji toggles was implemented too. 
+
+However, I haven't confirmed if the &#96;players&#96; slice is properly connected in the store. That's not crucial for now, but I need to ensure &#96;onOptInChange&#96; is typed correctly in &#96;TurnOrderListProps&#96;, which is good.
+
+Still, the user isn’t seeing the expected visual updates—green for active states. We’ve been using teal-500 instead of green-600. It's possible the commit hasn’t applied, and since the Setup page uses a different Toggle component, the user might only see the teal version. Adjusting it to green-600 could help!
+**Evaluating Toggle Positioning**
+
+The current implementation uses justify to position the knob container. The knob is centered, but I'm wondering about the span's width of 5 inside a 10 width parent. The class 'mx-0.5' indicates it's inline-block within a flex container. It should move based on justify-end, but it seems the input-based toggle previously used absolute translation instead. 
+
+The user likely isn’t seeing changes due to the Setup page not including &#96;racePotOptIn&#96;, which affects internal state toggling. I need to ensure everything is connected properly. I’ll run a TypeScript build and check for errors and run lints on the files I've changed.
+**Preparing Development Build**
+
+I’m thinking about running a development build or a preview now. In this environment, I can use a command like &#96;run_terminal_cmd&#96;. The pipeline suggests that I should run tests and build processes before wrapping up. 
+
+I'll start by running &#96;npm run build | cat&#96; to ensure everything compiles correctly, as we've made recent changes. Then I’ll run &#96;read_lints&#96; on the two files to check for issues. I also need to confirm that the &#96;TurnOrderList&#96; prop types match what’s being mapped. The &#96;avatarKey&#96; is required but not used, though I think its presence in &#96;SetupForm&#96; is fine. Time to run those lints!&lt;/details&gt;&lt;/think&gt;
+

+&lt;think&gt;&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Thought Process&lt;/summary&gt;
+**Planning Race Pot Implementation**
+
+I need to implement the Start Game deduction for the Race Pot and badge on the Play page since the user said "go." I’ll consider creating a race pot state, possibly in the session slice or a new pot slice. When starting the game, I should check if the number of opted-in players is two or more. If so, I'll compute the pot and deduct from their money, while also creating a badge UI to show the pot amount and participants. I might also want to track if the pot is active and who the winner is.
+**Defining Session State**
+
+I’m defining the SessionState in the session slice to include the current game ID and race pot information. I’ll add actions like initializeRacePot, which will set up the race pot at the game start, and awardRacePot for later use. I’ll need to deduct money from opted-in players only after confirming the game start. In SetupForm’s onStart, if there are two or more participants, I’ll adjust their money and initialize the race pot. Lastly, I’ll ensure the Play page displays the pot amount and participant count using the session data.
+**Implementing Race Pot Actions**
+
+I’m adding a new type and actions to extend the session slice to include the race pot. The racePot state will track whether it's active, the total amount, participants, winner ID, and whether it has been awarded. I’ll set up the reducers to initialize, award, and reset the race pot even if we only need to use initialize for now. In SetupForm’s onStart, I’ll derive participants and if there are two or more, I’ll dispatch the initialization and adjust player money. I also need to consider appending events for money adjustment and pot creation.
+**Adding Event and UI for Race Pot**
+
+I can simply append a MONEY_ADJUST event for each participant and maybe add a FEE or MISC event as well. The earlier prompt includes EventType union, and the code uses MONEY_ADJUST. I’ll create an event with a payload like "Race pot buy-in -100." Additionally, it’s necessary to display a badge on the PlayConsole using session.racePot and players. I’ll compute the display to show something like "🏃‍➡️💰 $&lt;amount&gt; (n participants)" at the top-right of PlayConsole. I'll make sure the Race Pot deduction only happens at the start to avoid duplication when re-entering Setup.
+**Implementing Setup and Race Pot Initialization**
+
+I need to ensure that when the user clicks the "Start Game" button, we navigate to /play responsibly. There’s a risk of double deducting money if they click multiple times, so I’ll disable the button after the first click. I’ll also check if session.racePot.active is true before deducing money to prevent re-initialization. In SetupForm, I’ll use useSelector to access this state and proceed only if there are enough participants. I’ll import necessary types and actions into sessionSlice, and add the pot badge UI in PlayConsole. It might be good to append events related to money adjustments as well.&lt;/details&gt;&lt;/think&gt;
+
+---
+
+I'll add race pot state and actions to the session slice, wire the deduction and initialization in the setup start handler, and add a pot badge in the Play Console. Then I'll run a build to verify.
+

-+ export interface CardDefinition {

-+   deck: CardDeck;
-+   text: string;
-+   effect: CardEffect;
-+ }
-+ 
-+ //#data

-+ export const COMMUNITY_CHEST: CardDefinition[] = [

-+   { id: 'c2', deck: 'community', text: 'Bank error in your favor. Collect $200', effect: { type: 'receiveBank', amount: 200 } },

-+   { id: 'c6', deck: 'community', text: 'From sale of stock you get $50', effect: { type: 'receiveBank', amount: 50 } },
-+   { id: 'c7', deck: 'community', text: 'Holiday fund matures. Receive $100', effect: { type: 'receiveBank', amount: 100 } },
-+   { id: 'c8', deck: 'community', text: 'Pay hospital fees of $100', effect: { type: 'payBank', amount: 100 } },
-+ ];
-+ 
-+ export const CHANCE: CardDefinition[] = [
-+   { id: 'h1', deck: 'chance', text: 'Advance to GO (Collect $200)', effect: { type: 'moveTo', tileId: 'go', awardGoIfPassed: true } },
-+   { id: 'h2', deck: 'chance', text: 'Advance to Illinois Avenue', effect: { type: 'moveTo', tileId: 'illinois-ave', awardGoIfPassed: true } },
-+   { id: 'h3', deck: 'chance', text: 'Advance to St. Charles Place', effect: { type: 'moveTo', tileId: 'st-charles-place', awardGoIfPassed: true } },
-+   { id: 'h4', deck: 'chance', text: 'Go to Jail. Do not pass GO', effect: { type: 'goToJail' } },
-+   { id: 'h5', deck: 'chance', text: 'Bank pays you dividend of $50', effect: { type: 'receiveBank', amount: 50 } },
-+   { id: 'h6', deck: 'chance', text: 'Pay poor tax of $15', effect: { type: 'payBank', amount: 15 } },
-+   { id: 'h7', deck: 'chance', text: 'Get Out of Jail Free', effect: { type: 'getOutOfJail' } },
-+   { id: 'h8', deck: 'chance', text: 'Advance token to nearest Utility', effect: { type: 'moveTo', tileId: 'electric-company', awardGoIfPassed: true } },
-+ ];
-+ 
-+ export const BUS: CardDefinition[] = [
-+   { id: 'b1', deck: 'bus', text: 'Bus Ticket: Choose any unvisited property in current or next set', effect: { type: 'busTicket' } },
-+   { id: 'b2', deck: 'bus', text: 'Bus Ticket: Choose any unvisited property in current or next set', effect: { type: 'busTicket' } },
-+   { id: 'b3', deck: 'bus', text: 'Bus Ticket: Choose any unvisited property in current or next set', effect: { type: 'busTicket' } },
-+ ];
-+ 
-+ //#helpers
-+ import { seededShuffle } from '@/lib/rng';
-+ 
-+ export function buildShuffledDeck(deck: 'chance' | 'community' | 'bus', seed: number | string): CardDefinition[] {
-+   const base = deck === 'chance' ? CHANCE : deck === 'community' ? COMMUNITY_CHEST : BUS;
-+   return seededShuffle(base, seed);
-+ }

-+   discardPile: CardDefinition[];

-+ 
-+ export interface CardsState {

-+     discardPile: [],
-+   };
-+ }
-+ 
-+ const cardsSlice = createSlice({
-+   name: 'cards',

-+   reducers: {

-+     putCardOnBottom(state, action: PayloadAction&lt;{ deck: CardDeck; cardId: string }&gt;) {

-+       }
-+     },
-+   },
-+ });

-@@ -1,1 +1,73 @@
-- 
-+ import type { DieRoll } from '@/types/monopoly-schema';
-+ import { BOARD_TILES, getTileByIndex, passedGo, GO_TO_JAIL_INDEX, JAIL_INDEX } from '@/data/board';
-+ 
-+ type AdvisoryType =
-+   | 'MOVE_TO'
-+   | 'TELEPORT_CHOOSE'
-+   | 'JAIL'
-+   | 'CAN_BUY'
-+   | 'PAY_RENT'
-+   | 'PAY_TAX'
-+   | 'DRAW_CARD'
-+   | 'NOTHING';
-+ 
-+ export interface Advisory {
-+   type: AdvisoryType;
-+   message: string;
-+   tileIndex?: number;
-+   tileId?: string;
-+   passedGo?: boolean;
-+ }
-+ 
-+ export interface EvaluateContext {
-+   currentIndex: number;
-+   isOwned: (tileId: string) =&gt; boolean;
-+   isOwnedByOther: (tileId: string) =&gt; boolean;
-+ }
-+ 
-+ export function evaluateRollAdvisories(roll: DieRoll, ctx: EvaluateContext): Advisory[] {
-+   const advisories: Advisory[] = [];
-+ 
-+   // Simple baseline: no triples/doubles precedence handled here — that will be part of a fuller engine.
-+   const totalSteps = roll.d6A + roll.d6B + (typeof roll.special === 'number' ? roll.special : 0);
-+   const targetIndex = (ctx.currentIndex + totalSteps) % BOARD_TILES.length;
-+   const tile = getTileByIndex(targetIndex);
-+ 
-+   // Go to Jail tile
-+   if (tile.index === GO_TO_JAIL_INDEX) {
-+     advisories.push({ type: 'JAIL', message: 'Go directly to Jail. Do not pass GO, do not collect $200.' });
-+     return advisories;
-+   }
-+ 
-+   // Movement
-+   advisories.push({
-+     type: 'MOVE_TO',
-+     message: &#96;Move to ${tile.name}.&#96; + (passedGo(ctx.currentIndex, targetIndex) ? ' Passed GO (+$200).' : ''),
-+     tileIndex: tile.index,
-+     tileId: tile.id,
-+     passedGo: passedGo(ctx.currentIndex, targetIndex),
-+   });
-+ 
-+   // Tile effects
-+   if (tile.type === 'property' || tile.type === 'railroad' || tile.type === 'utility') {
-+     if (!ctx.isOwned(tile.id)) {
-+       advisories.push({ type: 'CAN_BUY', message: 'Property is unowned. You may buy it.' });
-+     } else if (ctx.isOwnedByOther(tile.id)) {
-+       advisories.push({ type: 'PAY_RENT', message: 'Property owned by another player. Pay rent.' });
-+     } else {
-+       advisories.push({ type: 'NOTHING', message: 'You own this property.' });
-+     }
-+   } else if (tile.type === 'tax') {
-+     advisories.push({ type: 'PAY_TAX', message: 'Pay tax.' });
-+   } else if (tile.type === 'chance' || tile.type === 'community') {
-+     advisories.push({ type: 'DRAW_CARD', message: &#96;Draw a ${tile.type === 'chance' ? 'Chance' : 'Community Chest'} card.&#96; });
-+   } else if (tile.type === 'goToJail') {
-+     advisories.push({ type: 'JAIL', message: 'Go directly to Jail.' });
-+   } else if (tile.type === 'jail') {
-+     advisories.push({ type: 'NOTHING', message: 'Just Visiting.' });
-+   } else {
-+     advisories.push({ type: 'NOTHING', message: 'No immediate action.' });
-+   }
-+ 
-+   return advisories;
-+ }

-&#96;&#96;&#96;
+---
+
+_**User (2025-08-12 04:22Z)**_
+
+for the active player can we have options seperated into 3 sections,
+Pre-actions,
+Roll
+Post action. 
+Since Post actions is dependent on roll, lets have that part of the turn available after a confirmation of the roll.
+Have the dice default to none active.
+lets add Next to "Roll" the totall of the roll when DM selects it. 
+ASAF
 
 ---
 
-Got it: MVP will be GM-driven, tracking a live tabletop game. I started Milestone 2A accordingly.
+_**Agent (model gpt-5, mode Agent)**_
 
-What I implemented now:
-- Deck state management: &#96;cards&#96; slice tracks Chance/Community/Bus decks with seeded shuffling and draw/discard.
-- Advisory engine: basic evaluator that, given a roll and current index, suggests what happens (move target, pass GO, can buy, pay rent, pay tax, draw card, jail).
-- GM console: the &#96;Play&#96; page now accepts dice input (2d6 + special), evaluates, and shows advisories.
+&lt;think&gt;&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Thought Process&lt;/summary&gt;
+**Clarifying selection updates**
 
-Next steps I’ll do for GM flow:
-- Add pre-roll actions panel (buy/sell/mortgage) as buttons that log actions.
-- Add “Resolve tile” actions after advisories (buy property, pay rent/tax, draw specific card from deck and display its text).
-- Append events to the log and update current position so the next roll uses the new index.
+I’m considering displaying the sum in the section label after a selection update, maybe in parentheses. Movement might be part of the Update Turn. They want the roll included, so I’ll ensure we have a “Confirm Roll” button to set rollConfirmed to true, allowing movement later. The post-actions dropdown is currently a select, which I’ll keep hidden until confirmation. I also need to set the dice to default as none active, using null for updates.  
 
-- Built Milestone 2A foundation: card decks, advisory evaluator, and a minimal GM turn console in &#96;Play&#96;.
-- Ready to expand GM actions and event logging next.
+Now, "ASAF" could mean "Ask Something After Finishing," but they mentioned it before along with "Ask me anything first = AMAF." This is a bit confusing!
+**Finalizing roll total calculations**
 
----
+I'm ready to move forward! First, I need to ensure we compute the roll total correctly: special values can be numbers like 1, 2, 3, as well as "+1", "-1", or "Bus." The total should be calculated as d6A + d6B, adding any special numeric part, treating "Bus" as 0 for now. 
 
-_**User**_
+I should also update the 'Update Turn' button to disable it when d6A or d6B are null. However, should I require rollConfirmed to enable it? They want post-actions after confirmation, so I’ll review the pre/post display gating too. 
 
-all so not, that spectator mode, so other players can view the current progress of the game. How many houses left, property open, how much money individuel person has, what they own..
+Finally, I need to ensure TypeScript types allow null, especially for functions using d6A and d6B, guarding against nulls in onApplyMove.
+**Reviewing roll management**
 
----
+Alright, I’m going to check how the function evaluates the roll. I should adjust it to compute only if both dice are selected; otherwise, it won’t do anything. 
 
-_**Assistant**_
+Next to the "Roll" button, I’d like to display the total, showing something like "Roll (Total: X)" when both dice are selected; otherwise, it’ll just say "Roll." I can also manage the special selection as optional.

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

## Brief change list (to share with the team)

- **Property “…” details popup upgraded**: shows rent ladder with **house/hotel/skyscraper icons**, plus a **RENT multiplier line** (2× when owning all-but-1, 3× when owning full set) and highlights the **current effective rent**.
- **Details button always visible** on property rows (even when improved).
- **Mortgage behavior in popup**: mortgage action stays visible but becomes **disabled when the property has improvements**, with a tooltip explaining why.
- **UI polish**: group build controls now use **plus/minus icons** and have **hover/press/focus styles**.
- **Consistency**: replaced text “Close” buttons across modals/overlays with a reusable **`CloseIconButton`** (IoClose).

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Exploring jail label logic**

I’m thinking about how jailAttempts count failed attempts. For labeling inJail, it could be based on these attempts: 0, 1, or 2. For example, if there are 2 attempts, the next is "Last Roll"; 0 means "First Roll"; and 1 indicates "Second Roll." Now, the user wants labels in PlayerTurnCards too. We could use similar logic. For the active player, the jailChoice could state "Jail: Paying," while for non-jail it should indicate "Just Visiting." I could suggest alternatives to ensure clarity across the board!
**Refining jail logic for labels**

I’m considering how to adjust `getTileByIndex` for PlayerTurnCards to return a computed name based on the player or to modify PlayerTurnCards to accept the `getTileLabelForPlayer`. Since we’re in Ask mode, we’re only suggesting options, but the player card shows a positionIndex at the end. 

I should warn about the tricky case where a player can be in jail but still has a positionIndex of 13; they’ll move on a successful roll, but if they pay before moving, the label would be "Jail: Just Visiting 13," which seems fine! Also, if a player goes to jail, `inJail` is set to true, and `jailAttempts` reset to 0, leading to the label "Jail: First Roll."
**Suggesting jail label naming**

I’m thinking about how to name labels for jail status. Something like "Jail: Roll 1/3" is more compact and clear compared to "Jail: First Roll" or "Jail: Attempt 1/3." The term "Last Roll" suggests the player must pay afterward. 

In Mega rules, maxAttempts might vary depending on house rules, but the default is three. So we should align the label logic accordingly. I’ll suggest using ordinal strings and ensure `jailAttempts` is clamped between 0 to 2. If a player reaches three attempts, we treat it as "Last Roll" or "Paying." 

For any player still in jail with three attempts, it should show "Jail: Last Roll." Also, about the “Just visiting” part—active players could land there but are free before rolling, which is manageable. 

Now, I need to propose a label mapping table along with relevant suggestions and include code references for clarity!
**Providing jail label suggestions**

In Ask mode, I realize I can reference existing code if it's relevant, but since the user didn't specifically request to see it, it's better to keep my response concise. 

I suggest adding a label like "Jail: Freed" for players who get out by paying or using "get out of jail free" cards, though that might be unnecessary. I’ll focus on recommending relevant strings and a mapping approach.

I'll mention that the underlying board tile name remains static, and the UI already has access to `pl.inJail` and `pl.jailAttempts` as seen with the JailAttemptRibbon. I’ll also include some pseudo-code for clarity, but without any tool calls for now!</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

Your idea is solid, and it maps cleanly to the state you already have:

- `positionIndex === 13` (Jail tile)
- `player.inJail` boolean
- `player.jailAttempts` (failed attempts so far; you already use it for `JailAttemptRibbon`)

### Suggested labeling (simple + “smart”)
When `positionIndex === 13`:

- **If `!inJail`** → **`Jail: Just Visiting`**
- **If `inJail`**:
  - `jailAttempts === 0` → **`Jail: First Roll`**
  - `jailAttempts === 1` → **`Jail: Second Roll`**
  - `jailAttempts >= 2` → **`Jail: Last Roll`**

Then keep your index display the same way you do now (so it becomes e.g. **`Jail: Second Roll 13`**).

### Two small copy alternatives (even clearer / shorter)
- **Clear**: `Jail: Roll 1 of 3`, `Jail: Roll 2 of 3`, `Jail: Roll 3 of 3`
- **Compact**: `Jail: 1/3`, `Jail: 2/3`, `Jail: 3/3` (works well on mobile)

If you want it to feel extra “relationship-aware”, you can also show **`Jail: Freed`** for the moment right after they pay/use GOJF (when `inJail` flips false but they’re still physically on 13), but it’s optional—your “Just Visiting” label is already acceptable there.

_**User**_

right now for surfaces, i use bg-neutral-100 dark:bg-neutral-800 or bg-neutral-50  dark:bg-neutral-950 is there a best practice to make these surfaces light/dark color choices and have short cut for them.like..
level-0 =  bg-neutral-50  dark:bg-neutral-950 
level-1 = bg-neutral-100 dark:bg-neutral-800 

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

Yes—best practice is to stop sprinkling raw `bg-neutral-* dark:bg-neutral-*` everywhere and instead use **semantic “surface” tokens**. You’ve got a few good options (pick one and standardize).

## Option A (recommended): semantic utility classes via `@apply`
Create a small set of surface classes in `src/index.css` (or a `src/styles/surfaces.css` you import) and use them everywhere:

```css
@layer utilities {
  .surface-0 { @apply bg-neutral-50  dark:bg-neutral-950; }
  .surface-1 { @apply bg-neutral-100 dark:bg-neutral-900; }
  .surface-2 { @apply bg-white       dark:bg-neutral-800; }

  .surface-border { @apply border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-700; }
  .surface-text { @apply text-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100; }
  .surface-muted { @apply text-neutral-600 dark:text-neutral-300; }
}
```

Then your JSX becomes:

- `className="surface-1 surface-border surface-text rounded-xl"`

Pros: shortest in components, easy to tweak globally, still Tailwind-native.

## Option B: CSS variables as design tokens (most “design-system”)
Define `--surface-0/1/2` on `:root` and override under `.dark`, then use Tailwind arbitrary values:

- `bg-[color:var(--surface-1)]`

Pros: truly tokenized; can theme beyond just Tailwind neutrals later.

## Option C: a `cn` helper + constants (JS-only)
If you prefer keeping it in TS:

```ts
export const SURFACE = {
  0: 'bg-neutral-50 dark:bg-neutral-950',
  1: 'bg-neutral-100 dark:bg-neutral-800',
} as const;
```

Pros: no CSS work; Cons: less “global” than Option A/B.

### Quick recommendation
Go with **Option A** + include border/text tokens too. Surfaces without matching **border + text** tokens tends to drift and become inconsistent over time.

---

_**User**_

walk me through option A and how it will work with our current Text color ?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Explaining text color options**

The user asked about the current text color, so I need to discuss the code, like `text-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100`. I should clarify that it stays constant; `text-fg` maps to those values. For example, a wrapper can use `text-fg`, while inside, it can utilize `text-muted`. I must mention pitfalls like using `text-white` on colored backgrounds. I also need to cover surfaces that already use `bg-white dark:bg-neutral-900`. It’s important to provide a clear, step-by-step explanation.</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

### Option A (recommended): **semantic “surface + text” tokens** backed by CSS variables

The goal is: instead of repeating `bg-neutral-100 dark:bg-neutral-800` everywhere, you use **one semantic class** like `bg-surface-1`, and you **pair it with a semantic text color** like `text-fg` / `text-muted`.

Under the hood, those semantic classes resolve to different actual colors in light vs dark mode.

---

### How it would work in this project

#### 1) Define a small set of “surface levels”
Example set (you can tweak shades later):

- **surface-0**: app background  
  - light: `neutral-50`  
  - dark: `neutral-950`
- **surface-1**: raised panels/cards  
  - light: `neutral-100`  
  - dark: `neutral-900` (or `neutral-900/neutral-800` depending on contrast)
- **surface-2**: “inner” containers / sections inside panels  
  - light: `white` (or `neutral-50`)  
  - dark: `neutral-800`

So your shortcut becomes:

- `bg-surface-0`
- `bg-surface-1`
- `bg-surface-2`

…and you stop writing `dark:` for backgrounds most of the time.

#### 2) Define text tokens that don’t fight your current text usage
Right now your code commonly uses things like:
- `text-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100` (primary)
- `text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-200` (secondary)
- `opacity-70` etc (muted)

Option A just gives those a consistent name:

- **`text-fg`** → “primary text”
  - light: `neutral-900`
  - dark: `neutral-100`
- **`text-muted`** → “secondary text”
  - light: `neutral-600/700`
  - dark: `neutral-300/200`
- **`text-subtle`** → “tertiary”
  - light: `neutral-500`
  - dark: `neutral-400`

So your components can be written like:

- Panel: `bg-surface-1 text-fg`
- Subtext: `text-muted`
- Tiny meta: `text-subtle` or `opacity-70` (either works; token is just more consistent)

---

### The key point: **surface choice should NOT require changing text choice**
With Option A, “surface level” controls **background**, and your text tokens remain stable:

- `text-fg` should be readable on **all** `surface-*` backgrounds (that’s the contract).
- `text-muted` should still be readable (just lower emphasis).

So if today you do:

- `bg-neutral-100 dark:bg-neutral-800 text-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100`

Option A becomes:

- `bg-surface-1 text-fg`

Same idea, fewer classes, harder to accidentally mismatch.

---

### Where this helps immediately in your codebase
You have lots of places like:
- `bg-white dark:bg-neutral-900`
- `bg-neutral-100 dark:bg-neutral-800`
- `bg-neutral-50 dark:bg-neutral-950`

Those all become `bg-surface-*`, while **keeping your existing “fg vs muted” pattern**, just with consistent naming.

---

### One practical suggestion (so you don’t break anything)
If you adopt Option A, I’d keep your current explicit text colors during migration and only replace them gradually:

- Start by introducing `bg-surface-*` tokens first.
- Then optionally swap text colors to `text-fg/text-muted` where it’s easy.
- Leave special cases alone (e.g., colored property headers that intentionally use `text-white`).

If you want, I can outline the exact mapping table I’d use for `surface-0/1/2` and `fg/muted/subtle` so it matches the contrast you already like.

---

_**User**_

why name text-fg , text-muted, text-subtle vs text-primary, text-secondary, text-tertiary?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

### Why `text-fg / text-muted / text-subtle` vs `text-primary / text-secondary / text-tertiary`

Both work. The difference is **what they describe**:

#### `fg / muted / subtle` describes **contrast/emphasis**
- **`text-fg`**: “default readable foreground text on this surface”
- **`text-muted`**: “still readable, lower emphasis”
- **`text-subtle`**: “tertiary/meta, lowest emphasis that’s still intended to be readable”

This maps directly to how you *actually style today* (you often use `opacity-70`, `opacity-80`, etc.). It also stays correct if a surface changes—`text-fg` is always the “right” base text.

#### `primary / secondary / tertiary` describes **importance/rank**
That sounds nice, but it gets ambiguous fast:
- “Primary” could mean *brand primary color*, not text rank (people confuse `primary` with `text-blue-600` style concepts).
- “Secondary text” could be “disabled text” in some minds.
- When you add more roles later (danger, success, links), “primary” starts colliding conceptually.

### Practical reason in your app
You have many places with:
- `text-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100` (main)
- `text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-200` (supporting)
- plus opacity tweaks

Those are *contrast/emphasis decisions*, not “brand primary/secondary”.

### A hybrid naming that avoids the confusion
If you *prefer the rank wording*, I’d suggest:
- **`text-default`** (instead of `text-primary`)
- **`text-muted`**
- **`text-subtle`**

This keeps “muted/subtle” clarity while avoiding the “primary means brand” confusion.

If you tell me whether you already use “primary/secondary” elsewhere for buttons (e.g., primary button vs secondary button), I can recommend the least-confusing naming for this codebase.

## Option A implemented (semantic surface + text shortcuts)

### What I added
In `src/index.css` I introduced **CSS-variable backed utility classes**:

- **Surfaces**
  - `bg-surface-0` → `neutral-50` / `neutral-950`
  - `bg-surface-1` → `neutral-100` / `neutral-800`
  - `bg-surface-2` → `white` / `neutral-900`

- **Text**
  - `text-fg` → `neutral-900` / `neutral-100`
  - `text-muted` → `neutral-700` / `neutral-200`
  - `text-subtle` → `neutral-500` / `neutral-400`

These automatically follow your existing `.dark` class (from `ThemeManager`).

### Where I started using them (so you can see the pattern)
- `BuildSellOverlay` outer container/header now uses `bg-surface-2 text-fg` and `bg-surface-1`
- `SettingsModal` + `RulesModal` containers now use `bg-surface-2 text-fg`, and swapped a few `text-neutral-500 dark:text-neutral-400` to `text-subtle`

### How you’ll use it going forward
Instead of:

- `bg-neutral-100 dark:bg-neutral-800`

use:

- `bg-surface-1`

Instead of:

- `text-neutral-900 dark:text-neutral-100`

use:

- `text-fg`

Build passes.

_**User**_

i want to add, extentend bg-surface-3. where can i do that?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

Add it in **`src/index.css`**, in the same place we defined the other tokens:

- **Set the variable values** in both blocks:
  - `:root { ... --surface-3: ... }`
  - `.dark { ... --surface-3: ... }`

- **Expose the utility class** in the `@layer utilities` block:
  - `.bg-surface-3 { background-color: var(--surface-3); }`

You’ll see the existing definitions right now in `src/index.css` around the `--surface-0/1/2` variables and the `@layer utilities` section.

---

_**User**_

@src/index.css:26-27  i want to us this color but in 25% opacity. how do correctly do that?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

To make a token like:

```css
--surface-3: theme(colors.neutral.800);
```

be **25% opacity**, you have two good options.

### Option 1 (recommended): use Tailwind’s color-with-alpha helper
Tailwind supports `color-mix` via `theme(colors.X / Y%)`:

```css
--surface-3: theme(colors.neutral.800 / 25%);
```

That keeps it consistent with Tailwind’s color system.

### Option 2: use CSS `color-mix()` explicitly
If you want to be very explicit (and it works great with modern browsers):

```css
--surface-3: color-mix(in oklab, theme(colors.neutral.800) 25%, transparent);
```

### Quick note
If you’re defining this inside the `.dark { ... }` block, this makes a **translucent dark surface**. It will blend with whatever is behind it (your `bg-surface-0/1/2`), so make sure the layer underneath is what you expect.

---

_**User**_

@src/components/molecules/BuildSellOverlay.tsx:523-524 this area has bg colors, light and dark, that are not using our surfaces. how is it best delt with to addi to our surfaces?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

That row is a good example of a **“tinted overlay surface”** (semi‑transparent) sitting on top of a stronger colored parent (your group container) or a regular panel. That’s why it’s using `bg-white/50 dark:bg-neutral-900/50`: it’s not a flat “surface level”, it’s a *blend layer*.

### Best practice in your setup: introduce **tinted surface tokens** (separate from solid surfaces)
Keep your existing solid tokens:
- `bg-surface-0/1/2` = **opaque** backgrounds

Add a second family for translucent overlays, e.g.:
- **`bg-surface-tint`** (or `bg-surface-glass-1`)
- optionally `bg-surface-tint-strong` if you need two strengths

Then you can replace:
- `bg-white/50 dark:bg-neutral-900/50`
with:
- `bg-surface-tint`

### Why not just force it into `bg-surface-1`?
Because `bg-surface-1` is opaque and will **erase the colored group background** (your property color bands), while the current design intentionally lets that color show through.

### Concrete suggestion for this exact row
Create a token that matches the current intent:
- light: `white / 50%`
- dark: `neutral-900 / 50%` (or `neutral-950 / 40%` depending how heavy you want it)

Then the row becomes:
- `bg-surface-tint` (and you can keep `text-fg` or leave text as-is)

### Naming suggestion (consistent with Option A)
- `bg-surface-0/1/2/3` for **solid**
- `bg-surface-tint` (and maybe `bg-surface-tint-strong`) for **transparent overlays**

If you want, tell me where else you use `bg-white/.. dark:bg-neutral-../..` and I can propose a small set of tint tokens (usually 2–3) that cover 90% of cases without exploding your design system.

_**User**_

yes i think we need some some strenghts since we also have this one @src/components/molecules/BuildSellOverlay.tsx:522-523 and @src/components/molecules/BuildSellOverlay.tsx:531-532 

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

Yes—those two spots are perfect evidence you want **a small “tint/glass” scale**, separate from your solid `surface-*` scale.

### What those two cases represent
- **Row/card tint** (`bg-white/50 dark:bg-neutral-900/50`): a *medium* translucent panel that should let the group color show through.
- **Icon button tint** (`bg-white/80 dark:bg-neutral-900/60`): a *stronger* (more opaque) control surface so the button reads clearly on any background.

### Recommended approach: add “tint” tokens with 2–3 strengths
Keep your current solids:
- `bg-surface-0/1/2/3` (opaque)

Add a second family:
- **`bg-surface-tint-1`** (lightest)
- **`bg-surface-tint-2`** (your current `/50` style)
- **`bg-surface-tint-3`** (your current `/80` or `/60` style for controls)

Example mapping (close to what you already use):
- `bg-surface-tint-2`:
  - light: `white / 50%`
  - dark: `neutral-900 / 50%`
- `bg-surface-tint-3`:
  - light: `white / 80%`
  - dark: `neutral-900 / 60%`

Then you can replace:
- `bg-white/50 dark:bg-neutral-900/50` → `bg-surface-tint-2`
- `bg-white/80 dark:bg-neutral-900/60` → `bg-surface-tint-3`

### Bonus: do the same for borders (optional, but nice)
You also have repeated:
- `border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-700`
- `border-neutral-300 dark:border-neutral-700`

If you want consistency, define:
- `border-surface` (normal)
- `border-surface-strong` (for controls)

### Why this is “best practice” here
Because these aren’t “surface levels” (opaque layers). They’re **translucent overlays** that intentionally *blend* with whatever is behind them (property group colors, panels, etc.). Keeping them as a separate token family prevents confusion and avoids accidental design regressions.

If you want, tell me whether you prefer the naming `tint-*` vs `glass-*` vs `overlay-*`, and I’ll suggest the cleanest naming scheme to match `surface-*` and `text-*`.

## Added tint + border tokens (and wired them into `BuildSellOverlay`)

### New utilities in `src/index.css`
- **Tints**
  - `bg-surface-tint-2` (matches your old `bg-white/50 dark:bg-neutral-900/50`)
  - `bg-surface-tint-3` (matches your old `bg-white/80 dark:bg-neutral-900/60`)
- **Borders**
  - `border-surface` (old `border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-700`)
  - `border-surface-strong` (old `border-neutral-300 dark:border-neutral-700`)

### Updated the two spots you called out in `BuildSellOverlay`
- Property row container + header row now use:
  - `border-surface bg-surface-tint-2 text-fg`
- The “…” details button now uses:
  - `border-surface-strong bg-surface-tint-3`
  - hover/press via `hover:brightness-[1.03] active:brightness-[0.98]` (keeps it token-based)

_**User**_

why are we not using surface-tint-1?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

We *can* add `surface-tint-1`, but we didn’t yet because **we haven’t seen a real usage that needs a lighter tint than your existing `/50` and `/80` cases**.

### What we have today (from your UI)
- **Tint-2** matches `bg-white/50 dark:bg-neutral-900/50` → a “card/row overlay” tint
- **Tint-3** matches `bg-white/80 dark:bg-neutral-900/60` → a “control/button” tint with more contrast

Those cover the two strengths you pointed out in `BuildSellOverlay`.

### When `surface-tint-1` is useful
Add it when you find cases like:
- a *very subtle* hover background
- a *barely-there* divider panel that should show a lot of the parent color
- a “selected” state that shouldn’t overpower the base surface

Typical mapping would be something like:
- light: `white / 20–30%`
- dark: `neutral-900 / 20–30%`

### Design-system best practice
Start with the **minimum number of tokens** that cover real use-cases, then add levels only when needed—otherwise you end up with 6–8 variants that all look similar and people pick inconsistently.

If you want, tell me one or two spots where you currently use something like `bg-white/20` or `bg-neutral-900/30`, and I’ll suggest the exact `tint-1` values so it fits your current palette.

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

Yes—**that should be updated** for consistency.

That container is still using:

- `border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-700`

…but we now have the semantic token:

- `border-surface`

So the best practice is to replace the border classes there with `border-surface` (and keep the rest the same). That way when you ever tweak border colors globally, this component follows automatically.

You’ll likely find similar spots elsewhere (anywhere you see `border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-700` or `border-neutral-300 dark:border-neutral-700`)—those should generally become `border-surface` / `border-surface-strong` unless there’s a special reason (like colored property borders).

_**User**_

@src/components/molecules/BuildSellOverlay.tsx:740-741 how should we handle these dividers in our semantic tokens?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

Those “dividers” are basically **borders**, just applied via `divide-*` utilities instead of `border-*`. So the semantic-token approach is the same: introduce **divider tokens** that map to “hairline separators on surfaces”.

### Best practice: add divider tokens that mirror your border tokens
You already have:
- `--border` and `--border-strong`
- `border-surface` / `border-surface-strong`

Add equivalents for dividers:
- `--divider` (normal separators)
- `--divider-strong` (if needed)

Then expose utilities like:
- `divide-surface` (maps to `--divider`)
- `divide-surface-strong` (maps to `--divider-strong`)

### Why separate divider from border?
They often want the same *family* but slightly different tuning:
- dividers inside a panel can be a bit **lighter/subtler** than outer borders
- borders define component edges; dividers define **row separation**

You *can* point `--divider` to the same value as `--border` initially (simple), and later adjust if you want.

### How that applies to your snippet
Replace:

- `divide-neutral-200 dark:divide-neutral-800`

with something like:

- `divide-surface` (or `divide-surface-strong` if you want higher contrast)

### Recommended minimal token set
- `divide-surface` (most common)
- optionally `divide-surface-strong` (rare)

If you want, I can propose exact default values for `--divider` in light/dark that match your current `neutral-200` / `neutral-800` look, and keep everything consistent with `border-surface`.

_**User**_

@src/index.css:67-68 what is the logic not([hidden]) doing here?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

That selector is copying **Tailwind’s built‑in `divide-*` behavior**.

In Tailwind, `divide-y`/`divide-x` works by adding a border between *siblings*, but it **skips elements that are hidden** (either because they literally have the `hidden` attribute, or because React conditionally renders something that ends up hidden).

So this part:

- `> :not([hidden]) ~ :not([hidden])`

means:

- **`>`**: only look at **direct children** of the element you put `divide-surface` on.
- **`:not([hidden])`**: only consider children that **do not have the `hidden` attribute**.
- **`~`**: select every child that has a **previous sibling** (i.e., “not the first one”).

Net effect:

- It applies the divider border color to **every visible child except the first visible child**, creating separators **between** rows.
- If some children are `[hidden]`, they won’t create “phantom” dividers.

So it’s basically: “apply divider borders between visible siblings only.”

That row’s “bottom border” isn’t coming from `border-*` on the row—it’s coming from the **parent `divide-y` system**.

In the rent popup you now have something like:

- parent: `className="divide-y divide-surface"`
- children rows: `className="... px-3 py-2 ..."`

`divide-y` creates the 1px separators by applying **`border-top-width: 1px`** to each child after the first. Your `divide-surface` class sets the **`border-color`** for those borders via CSS.

### Why it might look “white”
Common reasons:
- The row background is `bg-surface-0` (very light), and the divider color (`--divider`) is also fairly light (`neutral-200`), so it can read as “white” depending on display.
- If another class is overriding divider color (e.g., a `border-white` somewhere higher specificity), it could mask it.

- Ensure the parent element has **both** `divide-y` and `divide-surface` (you already updated it to `divide-y divide-surface`).
- Confirm `--divider` is what you want (currently `neutral-200` in light mode). If you want stronger contrast, you’d use **`divide-surface-strong`** on that parent, not `border-surface` on the row.

### Best semantic fix for “make it use the right border color”
If you want that separator stronger/darker:
- Change the parent from `divide-surface` → **`divide-surface-strong`**.

That keeps the semantics correct: it’s still a “divider token”, just a stronger one.

If you want, paste the parent container line (the one with `divide-y ...`) and I can tell you exactly which token to use to get the visual you want.

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

That difference is exactly **light-mode vs dark-mode neutral tokens** expressed in **OKLCH** (Tailwind v4 uses OKLCH under the hood).

- **`oklch(0.97 0 0)`** is *very close to white* → that’s basically **`neutral-200` / light divider** territory (what your `--divider` is set to in light mode).
- **`oklch(0.269 0 0)`** is a *dark gray* → that’s basically **`neutral-800-ish`** territory (what your `--divider` is set to in dark mode).

So the divider is working; it just looks “white” in light mode because the token is intentionally subtle.

### If you want the divider to be more visible in light mode
You have two clean semantic choices:

- **Use a stronger divider on that container**: switch `divide-surface` → **`divide-surface-strong`** (maps to `--divider-strong`, currently `neutral-300` in light mode).
- **Make the default divider darker globally**: change `--divider` from `neutral-200` → `neutral-300` (or even `neutral-400`) in light mode. This affects all `divide-surface` usage.

Given your observation, I’d start with **`divide-surface-strong`** for that rent list (since it’s a data table-ish list where separators should be clearer) and keep `divide-surface` subtle for most other places.

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

To get **all borders in that little rent box** to be exactly `oklch(0.269 0 0)`, you have two clean options (one “quick”, one “design-system”).

### Option 1 (quick, local to this one spot): Tailwind arbitrary colors
On the wrapper (`border …`) and the divider container (`divide-y …`), set the color directly:

- Replace `border-surface` with an arbitrary border color:

```txt
border-[color:oklch(0.269_0_0)]
```

- Replace `divide-surface` with an arbitrary divide color (keep `divide-y`):

```txt
divide-[color:oklch(0.269_0_0)]
```

Notes:
- Tailwind class names can’t contain spaces, so you write OKLCH with underscores: `oklch(0.269_0_0)`.
- This will force that dark “ink” border **even in light mode**.

### Option 2 (recommended, consistent tokens): add a “high-contrast border/divider” semantic token
In `src/index.css`, define something like:

- `--border-contrast: oklch(0.269 0 0);`
- `--divider-contrast: oklch(0.269 0 0);`

…and expose utilities like:
- `border-contrast`
- `divide-contrast`

Then in your component you’d do:
- `border border-contrast`
- `divide-y divide-contrast`

This keeps your codebase consistent (you’re already moving toward semantic tokens), and you can later adjust “contrast borders” centrally if you change your design.

### One UX caution
`oklch(0.269 0 0)` is a **dark** neutral (close to `neutral-800`). It’s great for dark mode separators, but in light mode it will look *very heavy* (strong table gridlines). If that’s intentional for this rent ladder, it’s fine—just calling it out.

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

No—**with `divide-y` it’s not expected that every row has the same “bottom border color”**, because Tailwind’s divide system doesn’t draw bottom borders on each child.

### What `divide-y divide-surface` actually does
- It adds **`border-top-width: 1px`** to **every child *except the first***.
- Your `divide-surface` utility only sets **`border-color`** for those “not-first” children via:

- `> :not([hidden]) ~ :not([hidden]) { border-color: var(--divider); }`

So:
- The first row (`RENT`) has **no divider border at all** (its `border-top-width` is 0), so the `border-bottom-color` you see in DevTools is usually just a **computed default** and often irrelevant.
- The next rows *do* have `border-top-width: 1px`, so you’ll see the real divider color (in dark mode that’s your `--divider` → the darker OKLCH value).

### Why you’re seeing two different OKLCH values
You’re likely comparing:
- **Row 1 (`RENT`)**: `border-bottom-color` (but **border width is 0**, so color is not used)
vs
- **Row 2+**: `border-top-color` (and **border-top-width is 1**, so color is used)

To verify, inspect these on each row:
- `border-top-width` (this is the divider)
- `border-top-color` (should come from `--divider` when `divide-surface` is applied)

### If you want “true bottom borders” on every row
Then `divide-y` isn’t the right mechanism by itself. You’d instead do something like:
- give each row `border-b` and set a semantic border color class, or
- keep `divide-y` but add an explicit `border-t` on the first row / container so the “top line” exists too.

If you want, tell me whether you want **lines between rows only** (divide behavior) or **a bottom border under every row** (table/gridline behavior), and I’ll recommend the cleanest semantic-token approach for that.

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

What you’re *seeing* (a line between “RENT” and the next row) is still **the `divide-y` separator**, but DevTools can be confusing about **which element “owns” that line**.

### How `divide-y` draws the line
`divide-y` does **not** add a bottom border to every row. It adds a **1px border to only one side of the *next* sibling** (usually `border-top` on row 2+). Visually, that border sits *between* row 1 and row 2, so it looks like “row 1 bottom”.

### Why DevTools might show `border-bottom-width: 1px` on the RENT row
Two common causes:

1) **You’re inspecting the line visually**, but the line is actually **row 2’s `border-top`**.  
DevTools will still show a “bottom border” box model on the first row sometimes, but the authoritative truth is the **Styles panel**: look for the exact rule that sets the width.

2) **`divide-y-reverse` is in play somewhere**, which flips the divider from top to bottom. Tailwind implements `divide-y` with a `--tw-divide-y-reverse` variable. If that variable is `1`, the divider becomes **`border-bottom`** instead of `border-top` (still on row 2+, but it can look like it’s “owned” by row 1).

### Quick way to confirm what’s really happening
In DevTools, select **row 2 (the first non-RENT row)** and check:

- **Computed**: `border-top-width` and `border-top-color`
- **Styles**: which selector sets that width (`.divide-y > :not([hidden]) ~ :not([hidden]) { … }`)

If row 2 has `border-top-width: 1px`, then the divider is working normally and the “white” you see is just your current `--divider` value in that mode.

### If the goal is: “make that separator darker”
You don’t fix this by adding `border-surface` to the RENT row. You do it by changing the divider token usage:

- Use **`divide-surface-strong`** on the container (recommended for a rent table/list), or
- Change the **global** `--divider` to a darker neutral, or
- Create a special **contrast divider** token if you truly want `oklch(0.269 0 0)` even in light mode.

If you tell me whether you want that line darker **only in dark mode** or **in both modes**, I’ll point you to the cleanest token choice (`divide-surface-strong` vs a new “contrast divider” token).

The first child in a container might be something different, but I’m not totally sure. I wonder if the border color isn't showing because it's referencing the wrong property. It's confusing, but I will dive deeper to clarify this!
**Investigating border-color in dark mode**

I’m exploring an issue with the border-color from our divide-surface utility. It applies to all sides, but I'm not seeing it behave as expected. The rent row doesn't have a `border` class, but there are chip divs that do, although they don't seem relevant. The user mentioned dark mode, and I noticed the color isn’t what I expected—it's using a light value instead of a dark one. It makes me wonder if the CSS variables aren't set correctly in this context. I'll need to dig deeper!
**Examining border-color issues**

I’m looking into the border-color property not working as expected. It seems like the border-bottom-color might not be referencing the variable correctly. The divide-surface class is on the same container, but the custom CSS rule might not be applying due to nesting. It’s strange if the user sees divide-y styles but not divide-surface styles, possibly due to CSS load order or a default Tailwind setting. I see Tailwind v3 sets a default border-color, which could be causing confusion here. I need to dig deeper!
**Exploring CSS order issues**

I’m troubleshooting why the divide-surface override isn't applying as expected. It seems like without our override, Tailwind's default neutral-200 is showing. This could be related to the order of `@layer utilities`, where Tailwind's utilities might come after mine. Since I’m importing Tailwind first, it raises a question about layer merging. Tailwind v4 might generate the `.divide-y` utility later, resulting in it overriding my border-color rule. This is frustrating, and I need to figure out how to adjust the order properly!
**Clarifying border-color issues**

I'm analyzing why the border-color in my rule isn't applying. It seems that if a later rule from `.divide-y` includes a border-color, it could override mine. To fix this, I need to increase specificity, like using `.divide-y.divide-surface`. This matches against the default Tailwind `.divide-y`. My proposed solution is to update the CSS selectors for better specificity. I can guide the user to apply these adjustments or consider switching to Agent mode for implementation. It’s essential to ensure that the `divide-surface` class is on the same element as `divide-y`.</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

You’re diagnosing it correctly: **Tailwind’s `divide-y` utility sets a default divider `border-color`**, and your custom `divide-surface` rule currently **isn’t winning in the cascade**, so the divider falls back to that default light value (`oklch(0.97 0 0)`).

### Why it happens
- Tailwind’s generated `.divide-y > :not([hidden]) ~ :not([hidden]) { … border-color: … }` rule applies a default divider color (that light neutral).
- Our custom rule is:

```css
.divide-surface > :not([hidden]) ~ :not([hidden]) { border-color: var(--divider); }
```

That has **the same specificity** as Tailwind’s `.divide-y …` rule (both are “one class selector”), so **whoever is later in the final CSS wins**. In your build, Tailwind’s `.divide-y` rule is winning, so you see the default color.

### The fix (best practice)
Make the custom rule **more specific** so it overrides Tailwind regardless of ordering:

```css
.divide-y.divide-surface > :not([hidden]) ~ :not([hidden]) {
  border-color: var(--divider);
}

.divide-y.divide-surface-strong > :not([hidden]) ~ :not([hidden]) {
  border-color: var(--divider-strong);
}
```

(And if you also use `divide-x`, you’d add the same pattern for `.divide-x…`.)

This works because:
- Tailwind: specificity \(0,1,0\)
- Proposed: specificity \(0,2,0\) → always wins

### Quick confirmation step (in DevTools)
After that change, select the divider row and check:
- **Styles**: you should see the `.divide-y.divide-surface … { border-color: var(--divider) }` rule winning
- **Computed**: `border-*-color` should match your dark token value in dark mode.

If you switch back to Agent mode, I can apply this change cleanly in `src/index.css` (and keep `divide-surface` working everywhere).
