_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

You just proved the key detail: **your divider-color selector only targets “not-first” children** because of the `~` combinator.

So this behavior is expected:

- First child: does **not** match `:not([hidden]) ~ :not([hidden])` → your `divide-surface` color rule won’t apply to it.
- Second+ children: do match → they get your `--divider` color.

### Why you still see a 1px border on the “first” row
That means the “RENT” row has **its own border** coming from *some other rule* (not from the divide sibling rule), e.g.:
- a `border-b` / `border-y` class somewhere, or
- a rule that sets border width on all children (or a specific child), or
- `divide-y-reverse` behavior showing up as “bottom” in DevTools even though it’s conceptually the divider line.

In other words: **the separator line you see visually between row 1 and row 2 is conceptually the divider**, but DevTools can attribute it in a way that makes it look like “row 1 bottom”.

### The robust fix (covers your case)
Make `divide-surface` set border color on **all direct children**, not only the “not-first” ones. That way, *even if* the first row ends up with a border for any reason, it uses `--divider`.

Use both rules:

```css
/* Apply divider color to any borders on the rows */
.divide-y.divide-surface > :not([hidden]) {
  border-color: var(--divider);
}
.divide-y.divide-surface-strong > :not([hidden]) {
  border-color: var(--divider-strong);
}

/* Keep the classic divide behavior as well (optional, but explicit) */
.divide-y.divide-surface > :not([hidden]) ~ :not([hidden]) {
  border-color: var(--divider);
}
.divide-y.divide-surface-strong > :not([hidden]) ~ :not([hidden]) {
  border-color: var(--divider-strong);
}
```

- The first pair is what fixes *your* observed issue (first row border color).
- The second pair ensures the actual “divide line” between siblings is also forced correctly (often redundant once the first pair exists, but harmless and clear).

### Quick sanity check in DevTools
After that, on the “RENT” row:
- If it truly has `border-bottom-width: 1px`, you should now see `border-bottom-color` resolve to your `--divider` value.
- On row 2+, you should also see the divider line using `--divider`.

If you want, paste the exact computed rules that set `border-bottom-width: 1px` on the RENT row (the “Matched CSS rules” section). That will reveal exactly where that border width is coming from.

_**User**_

would this work ?
 border-color-white/10

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

Not as written.

### In Tailwind, the correct class form is:
- **`border-white/10`** (not `border-color-white/10`)

That sets the border color to white at 10% opacity.

### When it “works” vs when it doesn’t
- It **works** if you want a **fixed white** border (usually for dark backgrounds).
- It’s usually **not ideal** for your new semantic system, because it **won’t adapt** between light/dark surfaces.

### Better in your setup
Use the semantic tokens you added:
- `border-surface` / `border-surface-strong`
- `divide-surface` / `divide-surface-strong`

If you truly want “white 10% on dark only”, you can do:
- `border-neutral-900/10 dark:border-white/10` (or better: introduce a `border-on-dark` token).

---

_**User**_

for this border, i want to remain a variation of transparent white, regardles of light/dark mode. what is the best way to handle this with my Semantic tokens?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

If you want a border that is **always “transparent white”** (same in light + dark), treat it as a **special-purpose token**, not part of `border-surface`.

### Best semantic approach
Add a dedicated token + utility, e.g.:

- **Variable**: `--border-white-10: theme(colors.white / 10%);`
- **Utility**: `border-white-10` (or `border-glass` if it’s used for “glass/tint” elements)

Then you can use `border border-white-10` anywhere and it will be consistent.

### Why this is better than hardcoding `border-white/10`
- You keep the “single source of truth” in your design tokens.
- If later you want to change it to `/12%` or `/8%`, you change it once.

### Naming suggestion (fits what you’re building)
Since you already added “tint” tokens, a nice consistent name is:
- **`border-tint`** (or `border-glass`)
mapped to `white/10` (or whatever percent you like)

Use that when the border is meant to look like a *light reflection* rather than a structural divider.

### Rule of thumb
- **Structural borders/dividers** → `border-surface`, `divide-surface`
- **Decorative/glass borders** (always white-ish) → `border-tint` / `border-glass` (fixed white alpha)

@src/components/molecules/BuildSellOverlay.tsx:562-566 i want to sqeeze these images together a bit. in figfma i can use gap with negative number. but i dont think that will work withh css. how can do it?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

You’re right: **CSS `gap` can’t be negative**.

To “squeeze” icons together you have a few good options:

### Option A (best/cleanest): use **negative margins** between siblings
Replace `gap-2` with no gap, then give each icon (except the first) a negative left margin:

```tsx
<div className="flex items-center justify-center">
  <img className="h-7 w-7" ... />
  <img className="h-7 w-7 -ml-2" ... />
  <img className="h-7 w-7 -ml-2" ... />
</div>
```

In Tailwind you can tune it:
- `-ml-1`, `-ml-1.5`, `-ml-2`, etc.

### Option B: use Tailwind’s **negative space utilities**
Tailwind has `-space-x-*` which is exactly for this:

```tsx
<div className="flex items-center justify-center -space-x-2">
  <img className="h-7 w-7" ... />
  <img className="h-7 w-7" ... />
  <img className="h-7 w-7" ... />
</div>
```

This is basically a shortcut for “negative margins between children”.

### Option C (when overlap causes readability issues): keep tight spacing but add a tiny border/ring
If the icons overlap too much, add a subtle ring so edges stay readable:

- `ring-1 ring-white/40 dark:ring-black/20` (or similar)

That’s optional, but can look nice on tinted backgrounds.

If you tell me how much overlap you want (e.g. “about half the gap”), I can suggest the exact Tailwind value (`-space-x-1`, `-space-x-1.5`, `-space-x-2`, etc.).

---

_**User**_

the overlay behind our models are not stronge enough. where can i add some blur and up the density?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

### Where the “modal backdrop” is defined right now
In your app, each modal/overlay has its own backdrop `motion.div` / wrapper with classes like:

- `bg-black/40` (sometimes `/50`)

You’ll find them in (at least):
- `src/components/molecules/BuildSellOverlay.tsx`
- `src/components/molecules/RulesModal.tsx`
- `src/components/molecules/SettingsModal.tsx`
- `src/components/molecules/BoardPickerOverlay.tsx`
- `src/components/molecules/AuctionOverlay.tsx`
- `src/components/organisms/PlayConsole.tsx` (turn summary / card picker / teleport picker overlays)

Look for `className="fixed inset-0 ... bg-black/40 ..."`.

### How to make it stronger (density + blur)
On those backdrop wrappers, bump opacity and add a backdrop blur:

- **Density**: change `bg-black/40` → `bg-black/60` (or `/70`)
- **Blur**: add `backdrop-blur-sm` or `backdrop-blur-md`

Example class idea:

- `className="fixed inset-0 bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm ..."`

If you want it to feel extra “frosted”, use:
- `bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-md`

### Best practice so you don’t repeat this everywhere
Create a single semantic utility class in `src/index.css`, e.g.:
- `.modal-backdrop` (or `bg-backdrop`)

and apply that class to all modal backdrops. That lets you tune blur/opacity once and all modals update together.

If you tell me which overlay(s) you want to update first (all modals vs just one), I can point you to the exact wrapper lines to change.

_**User**_

@src/components/molecules/BuildSellOverlay.tsx:550-561 @src/components/molecules/BuildSellOverlay.tsx:567-578  i want to give thes buttons a bit more emphasis. we have a "Confirm" emerald green primary button already so im thinking more of an amber button. what do you think?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

Amber is a good choice here, and it won’t compete with your **emerald “Confirm”** as long as you keep it **outline/soft-fill** (not a big solid amber block).

### Recommendation: “secondary accent” amber (outline + subtle fill)
This reads as “important control” without becoming “primary action”.

**Light mode**
- Border: `border-amber-400`
- Text/icon: `text-amber-800`
- Hover: `hover:bg-amber-50`
- Active: `active:bg-amber-100` (or keep your `active:scale`)

**Dark mode**
- Border: `dark:border-amber-500/60`
- Text/icon: `dark:text-amber-200`
- Hover: `dark:hover:bg-amber-900/20`

That gives emphasis + keeps the button “buttony” in both themes.

### Alternative (even cleaner): icon-only emphasis
Since these are just plus/minus icons, you can also emphasize by:
- making them slightly larger (`h-4.5 w-4.5` / `h-5 w-5`)
- adding a faint background by default (`bg-amber-50/60 dark:bg-amber-900/10`) but **still not solid**

### One caution
Avoid a fully solid amber background (`bg-amber-500`) for both buttons—two bright primary-looking buttons next to each other will visually fight your emerald primary CTA and make the row feel “loud”.

If you want, tell me whether you prefer “outline” or “soft filled”, and whether the buttons should be the same style when disabled (usually keep border muted + reduce opacity).

---

_**User**_

@src/components/molecules/BuildSellOverlay.tsx:426-427 in the modal i do want a t column property cards when there are more then 2 but one there are single or uneven the cards should be centered. how can we fix?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

You can solve this cleanly with **CSS grid “auto-fit/auto-fill” + `place-content-center`**, instead of hard-coding `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2`.

### The goal you described
- If there are **3+ cards**: show **2 columns** (your “two column property cards”)
- If there are **1 card**: it should be **centered**
- If there are **odd counts** (3, 5, …): last card shouldn’t awkwardly stick left; ideally the grid block is centered.

### Best pattern (Tailwind)
1) Use a grid that can make up to 2 columns, but centers leftover space:

- Replace `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2` with:

- `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 place-content-center`

`place-content-center` centers the whole grid content within the container, which helps when the last row isn’t full.

2) Center single items explicitly:
If you want a single card to be centered in a 2-col grid, you can add:

- `justify-items-center` (centers items in their grid cell)
- AND set a max width on cards (so they don’t stretch full-width)

But your cards currently stretch to fill; so you need either:
- a max-width wrapper for each card (e.g. `max-w-md w-full`), or
- set grid columns to a fixed width so single cards don’t span the whole row.

### Even better (more “responsive” and always centered): `auto-fit` with `minmax`
Use Tailwind’s arbitrary grid template:

```txt
grid [grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(320px,1fr))] justify-center
```

Then cap to 2 columns by controlling the container width (or add a breakpoint-specific template). Example:

- base: 1 column
- `sm+`: auto-fit with min width
- optional: set `max-w-[calc(2*320px+gap)]` on the grid container to prevent 3 columns

This gives you:
- 1 card: centered (because the grid doesn’t stretch beyond its content if you also use `mx-auto` + max width)
- 2+ cards: fills to 2 columns when space allows

### Practical recommendation for your current layout
Simplest minimal change likely to satisfy you:

- Keep 2 columns on `sm`+
- Add `place-content-center`
- Add `mx-auto` and a `max-w-*` to the grid container so it doesn’t span the entire modal width when there’s only 1 column of content

Example idea (not editing, just concept):
- Grid container: `mx-auto w-full max-w-3xl grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 gap-3 place-content-center`

If you tell me the typical card width you want (e.g. “each card about 360px”), I can give you the exact `minmax(...)` values and `max-w-[...]` you’d use so it always stays 2-col max and centers perfectly.

@src/components/molecules/StepNavigator.tsx:20-21  is stripping the numeric suffix the best may to have not show? should'nt we just not have it at all? or is needed because ou turnRibbon?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

Stripping the numeric suffix is **a pragmatic UI-layer fix**, but **it’s not the “cleanest” architecture** if you can avoid generating the number in the first place.

### Why stripping is OK
- `StepNavigator` is a **pure presentational component**: it renders whatever “step title” it gets.
- If multiple callers might pass `Pre 1`, `Roll 2`, etc., doing a small normalization here guarantees the UI stays consistent without hunting down every producer.
- It also future-proofs a bit (if a title comes in as `Turn 3 — Roll 1`, it still displays nicely).

### Why “don’t include it at all” is cleaner
If the only place step titles are created is `PlayConsole` (or one central place), then the best practice is:
- **Make the data correct at the source**: titles should be `Pre`, `Roll`, `Post`—no numbers.
- Then `StepNavigator` doesn’t need to “guess” what part of the string is metadata.

### Is it needed because of `TurnRibbon`?
No—`TurnRibbon` showing turn/roll context doesn’t require the step titles to have numbers. In fact, it’s the opposite: since `TurnRibbon` is the “turn context”, step titles can stay simple.

### My recommendation for your codebase
- **Short term**: the stripping approach is fine (low risk).
- **Long term**: move the responsibility to the creator of `stepItems` (likely in `PlayConsole`) and keep `StepNavigator` dumb.

If you want, tell me where `stepItems` is built (it’s in `PlayConsole.tsx` somewhere) and I’ll point to the exact spot to remove the numeric suffix at the source so you can delete the regex entirely.

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

Right now this can’t work as described because **`preAction` is never set to anything other than `"None"`** in `PlayConsole` (there are no `setPreAction(...)` calls besides the reset), so the StepNavigator will always show `"None"`.

### Best “long-term” fix (source-of-truth in `PlayConsole`)
1) **Change `preAction` to mean “what the player actually did”**, and let “nothing yet” be empty:

- Initialize/reset it to `''` (empty string) instead of `"None"`.

2) Compute the StepNavigator “Pre” description like this:

- **Before leaving Pre (start of turn)**: show **`—`** if `preAction` is empty.
- **After leaving Pre** (player hit Next at least once): show **`None`** if still empty.

In code, that logic is typically:

```ts
const preDesc =
  highestStep === 0 && !preAction
    ? '—'
    : (preAction || 'None');
```

(Using `highestStep` is nice because if the user navigates back to Pre after advancing, it will still show `None`, matching your rule.)

3) Actually set it when something happens
Example triggers:
- When player opens/uses Manage: `setPreAction('Managed')` (better on **confirm** than on open)
- If you later add other pre-actions: set strings like `Depot installed`, `Mortgage toggled`, etc.

### Why not do this in `StepNavigator`?
`StepNavigator` should stay dumb: it renders `items`. The “— vs None” rule depends on **turn progression state** (`highestStep` / `activeStep`) which belongs in `PlayConsole`.

If you want, I can point to the exact reset line and `stepItems` block to change (it’s around where you currently build `stepItems` in `PlayConsole`).

---

_**User**_

step 3 sounds the best. We will need to build "deal" pre action where a user can make a deal with another player in the beginnng of there turn. 

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

Yes—“step 3” is the right direction, and “Deal” fits naturally as a **Pre-action**.

### How I’d model it
- **`preAction` should represent “what happened in Pre this turn”**.
- Start of turn: `preAction = ''` (means “nothing yet”)
- If player does something in Pre:
  - `setPreAction('Managed')` when they **confirm** a Manage change
  - `setPreAction('Dealt')` (or `Deal made`) when they **confirm** a deal

If you later want multiple actions, make it a list:
- `preActions: string[]` and display `preActions.join(', ')` (or “Managed + Deal”)

### The “— then None” display rule
You’ll use turn progression (like `highestStep`) to decide:
- **At very start (still in Pre, nothing yet)** → show **`—`**
- **After leaving Pre (still nothing happened in Pre)** → show **`None`**

### For the upcoming Deal feature
When you build the Deal modal:
- On open: don’t set anything yet (user might cancel)
- On confirm: update game state + `setPreAction('Dealt')` (or push into the list)

If you want, tell me whether “Manage” and “Deal” can both happen in the same turn (probably yes). If yes, I’d recommend switching now to `preActions: string[]` so you don’t have to refactor again later.

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

Then I’d switch from a single string to a **list** now:

### Recommended shape
- **State**: `preActions: string[]` (start empty each turn)
- **Helper**: `addPreAction(label)` that de-dupes:

```ts
setPreActions((prev) => (prev.includes(label) ? prev : [...prev, label]));
```

### Display rule (matches your “— then None”)
Let `preText` be:

- **At start of turn (still Pre, nothing logged yet)**: `—`
- **After you’ve moved past Pre (still nothing logged)**: `None`
- **If actions exist**: `Managed, Dealt` (or `Managed + Deal`)

Conceptually:

- if `preActions.length > 0` → show `preActions.join(', ')`
- else if `highestStep === 0` → `—`
- else → `None`

### When to append
- **Manage**: add only when the user **confirms** changes (not when opening the overlay).
- **Deal**: add only on **confirm** (not on open/cancel).

This keeps the StepNavigator truthful and avoids “false positives” when someone opens a modal and backs out.

Implemented the **Pre-action log list** + the **“— then None”** behavior.

### What changed
- **`preAction` → `preActions: string[]`** in `PlayConsole`
  - Resets to `[]` at the start of each turn.
  - Added `addPreAction(label)` helper (dedupes).

- **StepNavigator “Pre” label rule**
  - If `preActions.length > 0` → shows e.g. `Managed` (or later `Managed, Dealt`)
  - Else if you’re still at the very start of the turn (`highestStep === 0`) → shows **`—`**
  - Else (you’ve moved past Pre with nothing done) → shows **`None`**

- **Manage tracking**
  - When `BuildSellOverlay` is **confirmed**, we now log **`Managed`** via `addPreAction('Managed')` (so canceling doesn’t count).

Build passes.

_**User**_

in the @src/components/molecules/StepNavigator.tsx i want the pre labels, number circle, to be aligned left to card and the Post section aligned right. Currentyl they are centered. 

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

You can do this by changing the **alignment styles on each step column** based on whether it’s the first/last item.

Right now each step uses:

- `className="flex-1 flex flex-col items-center text-center"`

That forces everything to center.

### What to change conceptually
For each step, compute alignment from the index:

- **Pre (first)**: left aligned
  - container: `items-start text-left`
  - circle wrapper: `justify-start`
- **Roll (middle)**: keep centered
  - `items-center text-center`
- **Post (last)**: right aligned
  - `items-end text-right`
  - circle wrapper: `justify-end`

### Where in the file
In `StepNavigator.tsx`, inside `items.map((s, i) => …)`:
- Replace `items-center text-center` on the outer step wrapper with a conditional class based on `i === 0` / `i === items.length - 1`.
- Also adjust the circle row (`<div className="relative w-full h-8 flex items-center justify-center">`) so it uses `justify-start / justify-center / justify-end` the same way.

### Connector lines caveat
Your connector bars are absolutely positioned from left/center/right, so they’ll still draw correctly even if the content aligns left/right. But if you feel the circle drifting too far, you can add a tiny horizontal padding (`px-1`) to the column wrapper so it doesn’t hug the edge.

If you want, I can give the exact Tailwind class snippet for the `alignClass` and `justifyClass` variables you’d drop into the map loop.

Since the circle has a fixed width of 32px, I realize the left edge aligns with the container’s left. I think I want to start the line from the circle's center, which means starting at 16px. I'll need to set CSS properties accordingly and adjust StepNavigator.
**Implementing connector logic**

I'm thinking about how to set up connectors inside a mapping function. I determine if I'm dealing with the first or last item, which helps decide alignment and justification in CSS. For the connectors, the left segment should appear when the index is greater than zero, connecting from the left to the circle's center. 

For the last item case, I need to adjust the calculations, setting the right positioning properly. In the middle case, the circle should be centered, while for the first, no left connector is needed.
**Setting up connector cases**

I’m figuring out how to position connectors based on whether an item is the first, middle, or last. For the first item, the circle aligns left, so I set the left distance to `calc(16px)` and the right to zero. In the middle, the left is set to 50%. For the last item, there's no connector. 

Progress connectors also need attention: the left shows when the index is within the active step. I’ll make sure the baseline connectors are visible behind the progress ones and then apply the patch before running the build.</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

You already *have* most of the behavior—`DiceSelector` is wrapped in a container that dims/disables when `busTeleportTo != null`:

```1600:1604:src/components/organisms/PlayConsole.tsx
<div data-ql="bus-dice-teleport-container" className={busTeleportTo != null ? 'opacity-50 pointer-events-none p-4' : 'px-4 py-1'}>
  <DiceSelector ... />
</div>
```

What you’re asking for is mainly a **UI refactor** in the **Roll step** (`activeStep === 1`) to match the “section card” pattern you already use in Post (`rounded-lg border …` + small uppercase label).

### What to do (structure)
In `PlayConsole.tsx`, inside the `activeStep === 1` block (lines ~1592+), restructure it like:

- **Section card: “Roll”**
  - Contains the `DiceSelector` and “Total” chips
- **OR separator**
- **Section card: “Use Bus”** (only when `available > 0`)
  - Contains the existing “Use Bus Ticket” button (and the “selected bus destination” pill when `busTeleportTo != null`)

### How to do “dim the opposite”
Define two booleans in that roll step render:

- **`busChosen`**: `busTeleportTo != null`
- **`diceChosen`**: depends on your rules:
  - normal: `d6A != null && d6B != null && special != null`
  - in-jail “Try Doubles”: `d6A != null && d6B != null` (since you hide special)

Then:
- **Roll section** gets dimmed when `busChosen`
- **Use Bus section** gets dimmed when `diceChosen`

Use the same pattern you already use: `opacity-50 pointer-events-none` on the inactive section.

### “OR” separator
Between the two cards, add a small row like:
- `OR` centered with thin lines left/right (or just a pill).

### Concrete JSX sketch (drop-in concept)
(This is *proposed code* showing the idea.)

```tsx
const busChosen = busTeleportTo != null;
const pid = players[turnIndex]?.id || players[0]?.id;
const pl = players.find((x) => x.id === pid);
const inJailRolling = pl?.inJail && jailChoice === 'roll';
const diceChosen = inJailRolling ? (d6A != null && d6B != null) : (d6A != null && d6B != null && special != null);

const rollInactive = busChosen;
const busInactive = diceChosen;

<div className="space-y-3">
  <div className={`rounded-lg border border-surface bg-surface-2 p-3 ${rollInactive ? 'opacity-50 pointer-events-none' : ''}`}>
    <div className="text-[11px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-subtle">Roll</div>
    <div className="mt-2">
      <DiceSelector ... />
      {/* total / doubles / triples chips */}
    </div>
  </div>

  {available > 0 && (
    <>
      <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
        <div className="h-px flex-1 bg-surface-1" />
        <div className="text-[11px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-subtle">OR</div>
        <div className="h-px flex-1 bg-surface-1" />
      </div>

      <div className={`rounded-lg border border-surface bg-surface-2 p-3 ${busInactive ? 'opacity-50 pointer-events-none' : ''}`}>
        <div className="text-[11px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-subtle">Use Bus</div>
        <div className="mt-2">
          {/* your existing “Use Bus Ticket” / “✓ 🚐 to …” UI */}
        </div>
      </div>
    </>
  )}
</div>
```

### One more “best UX” tweak
When a user *chooses bus*, you should clear dice selections (and vice versa) so the UI never ends up in a weird “both chosen” state:

- On `Use Bus Ticket` click: `setD6A(null); setD6B(null); setSpecial(null);`
- On any dice select: `setBusTeleportTo(null);` (and maybe close the center overlay)

If you want, tell me whether “Use Bus” should appear when:
- they have bus tickets in inventory **only**, or
- also when the **Bus special die** is rolled (that’s a different mechanic in your rules), and I’ll map it cleanly to the existing state you already have.

I can make this change, but your message came in while **Ask mode** is active (I can’t edit files in Ask mode). If you switch back to **Agent mode**, I’ll implement it.

Meanwhile, here’s the exact plan to match your rule + UI:

## What to change (`src/components/organisms/PlayConsole.tsx`)

### 1) Only show “Use Bus” when it’s allowed (inventory + after doubles extra roll)
In the `activeStep === 1` (Roll) section, compute eligibility like:

- **Inventory only**: `availableTickets = busTicketsAvailableThisTurn + stagedBusTickets`
- **Only on a following roll**: `rollCount > 1`
- **Only if the previous confirmed roll was doubles** (and not triples):

Conceptually:

```ts
const availableTickets = busTicketsAvailableThisTurn + stagedBusTickets;

const prevSeg = turnSegments.find((s) => s.roll === rollCount - 1);
const prevIsDoubles = !!prevSeg && prevSeg.d6A != null && prevSeg.d6B != null && prevSeg.d6A === prevSeg.d6B;

// triples are also doubles, but should NOT allow an extra roll / bus replacement
const prevIsTriple =
  prevIsDoubles &&
  typeof prevSeg?.special === 'number' &&
  prevSeg.special === prevSeg.d6A;

const canUseBusInsteadOfRoll = availableTickets > 0 && rollCount > 1 && prevIsDoubles && !prevIsTriple;
```

Then render the **Use Bus** section only when `canUseBusInsteadOfRoll` is true.

This matches your example:
- Roll 1: `2,2,bus` → doubles => Roll 2 exists
- Roll 2: bus section appears, player can use bus ticket instead of rolling

### 2) Add the “Roll” section card styling + OR separator
Wrap `DiceSelector` in a section card styled like your Post sections:

- container: `rounded-lg border border-surface bg-surface-2 p-3`
- label: `text-[11px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-subtle`

Add an “OR” separator between the two section cards.

### 3) Mutually exclusive + dim the opposite side
Define:

- `busChosen = busTeleportTo != null`
- `diceChosen = inJailRolling ? (d6A!=null && d6B!=null) : (d6A!=null && d6B!=null && special!=null)`

Then:
- If `busChosen`: dim dice section (`opacity-50 pointer-events-none`)
- If `diceChosen`: dim bus section (`opacity-50 pointer-events-none`)

### 4) Make choosing one clear the other (correctly restore tickets)
You already **consume** a bus ticket when a bus destination is chosen, and you already have a “cancel bus selection” button that restores it. When you clear `busTeleportTo` due to dice selection, you should do the **same restore logic** (grant ticket back + increment `busTicketsAvailableThisTurn`) so inventory stays correct.

---

If you switch to **Agent mode**, I’ll implement the refactor in the Roll step UI and make sure the ticket restore/consume logic stays consistent.

Implemented the Roll UI refactor + the bus rule.

### Roll step UI (activeStep === 1)
- Wrapped **DiceSelector** in a section card with a **“Roll”** label (same visual style as your Post sections).
- Added a **“Use Bus”** section card with an **OR** divider between them.
- **Mutual exclusivity + dimming**:
  - If a bus destination is chosen, the Roll section becomes dimmed/inactive.
  - If dice are chosen, the Use Bus section becomes dimmed/inactive.

### Bus rule enforced
**Use Bus** only appears when:
- Player has **bus tickets in inventory** (`busTicketsAvailableThisTurn > 0`)
- It’s a **following roll** (`rollCount > 1`)
- The **previous roll was doubles**
- The previous roll was **not triples** (triples don’t create an extra roll)

### Switching restores state correctly
- If a bus destination was selected and you start selecting dice, it **cancels the bus selection**, restores the ticket, and clears `predictedTo`.
- If you choose “Use Bus Ticket”, it clears current dice selections before opening the bus destination picker.

Build passes.

_**User**_

@src/components/molecules/DiceSelector.tsx:22-23 is there way we can make the gap flexible with a min max size?

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.2)**_

Yes, but **not with `gap` itself**. In CSS, `gap` is just a fixed length; there’s no `minmax()` for `gap`.

### Best way: use `clamp()` for a responsive gap
Use Tailwind’s arbitrary value syntax to set a gap that grows/shrinks within bounds:

```tsx
<div className="grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3 gap-[clamp(0.5rem,2vw,1.25rem)]">
```

That means:
- minimum: `0.5rem`
- fluid: `2vw`
- maximum: `1.25rem`

You can tweak those numbers to taste.

### Alternative: keep `gap-*` and change it by breakpoint
Simple and very Tailwind-y:

```tsx
<div className="grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3 gap-3 sm:gap-4 lg:gap-5">
```

### If you meant “columns should be min/max sized”
That’s `grid-template-columns` with `minmax()`, e.g.:

```tsx
<div className="grid [grid-template-columns:repeat(3,minmax(0,1fr))] gap-4">
```

…but in your case `sm:grid-cols-3` already does effectively that.

If you tell me your desired min/max gap (e.g. “8px–20px”), I can give you the exact `clamp()` values.

-
-### Tech plan aligned to the brief
-
-Based on &#96;docs/Project Brief.md&#96;, here’s a concise, practical stack you can build quickly and grow into multiplayer. I’m offering options and asking a few clarifying questions as you prefer [[memory:431802]].
-
-### Recommended (pro dev choice)
-- React + TypeScript + Vite
-- Redux Toolkit (RTK) with Immer, React-Redux
-- RTK Query (future-proof for API/multiplayer; can be idle in MVP)
-- React Router
-- Tailwind CSS v4
-- Zod + react-hook-form (validations and UI forms for setup)
-- redux-persist + localForage (IndexedDB) for save/load and resilience
-- Vitest + React Testing Library
-- ESLint + Prettier + Tailwind plugin
+_**Agent (model gpt-5, mode Agent)**_
 
-Why: Fast DX with Vite + TS, familiar Redux patterns, smooth evolution to server sync via RTK Query, strong local persistence for MVP, and Tailwind 4 for rapid UI.
+&lt;think&gt;&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Thought Process&lt;/summary&gt;
+**Clarifying game rules**
 
-### Easy (fastest MVP)
-- React + TS + Vite
-- Redux Toolkit only (no RTK Query yet)
-- Tailwind 4
-- redux-persist + localStorage (simpler than IndexedDB)
-- Minimal validations (Zod optional)
-- Pros: You can ship dice, turns, undo, timeline, save/load quickly.
-- Cons: You’ll rewire persistence and data boundaries later for multiplayer.
+For the third roll, if countDoublesInCurrentTurn equals 2 and a double or triple is present, then it leads to jail. The special case for triples is already included under 'DOUBLE_DOUBLE_TRIPLE.' I’ll clarify that the jail rule for triple ones is overridden by this point. There's no need to modify code further since it's mainly about conceptual clarity. I’ll structure this with bullet points and keep it concise while adhering to Markdown guidelines.
+**Reiterating game rules**
 
-### Scalable (multiplayer-first)
-- React + TS + Vite (or Next.js if you want built-in API routes)
-- Redux Toolkit + RTK Query
-- Tailwind 4
-- Backend:
-  - Recommended serverless: Supabase (Postgres, Realtime, Auth)
-  - Self-hosted: Fastify/Express + Prisma + Postgres + Socket.IO
-- Pros: Realtime sync, auth, cloud persistence from day 1.
-- Cons: Slightly more upfront setup.
+I’ll clarify the rules: if there are two doubles followed by a triple, the player goes to jail. The triple counts as a double for this logic on the third roll, with no bonuses involved. During a turn, I’ll start with a streak of 0; each roll either increments the streak for doubles or resets it. If the streak becomes 3, it leads to jail. Importantly, triples never grant an extra roll and end the turn, addressing the issue about consecutive triples.
+**Clarifying game mechanics**
 
-### Data and state model
-- Use an event log as the source of truth to power “Undo last action” and the “timeline summary”.
-  - Store append-only &#96;GameEvent&#96; records (roll, move, purchase, rent, tax, card, bus, pot changes, winner).
-  - Derive current &#96;Game&#96; state via selectors that fold the event log. Cache snapshots if needed for performance.
-- Keep Redux slices focused by feature:
-  - &#96;gameEvents&#96; (event log, IDed by UUID, timestamps, actor)
-  - &#96;ui&#96; (dialogs, toasts, selected player/tile)
-  - &#96;session&#96; (current game ID, loaded save, multiplayer status)
-- For undo: either
-  - Add a &#96;revert_to&#96; event (authoritative rollback), or
-  - Maintain &#96;past/present/future&#96; using Redux with Immer. Event-sourced &#96;revert_to&#96; scales better for multiplayer and the summary.
+I think we should clarify if a triple counts as a "double" during the first or second rolls. Since the turn ends regardless, it's not necessary to link it to the double streak. Instead, we'll treat triples as non-affecting regarding the double streak. For jail detection, we'll monitor consecutive doubles. If there are two doubles and the current roll is either a triple or double, the player goes to jail. Otherwise, if it's a triple, we'll handle it, end the turn, and reset the count.
+**Finalizing game rules**
 
-### Multiplayer strategy (for “Multiplayer sync”)
-- Supabase approach (recommended):
-  - Tables: &#96;games&#96;, &#96;game_events&#96;, &#96;players&#96;
-  - Realtime on &#96;game_events&#96; filtered by &#96;game_id&#96;
-  - Optimistic local append; server validates and broadcasts
-  - Access via RTK Query endpoints
-  - Auth: anonymous or magic link (later)
-- Self-hosted:
-  - Fastify/Express + Postgres + Prisma
-  - Socket.IO rooms per &#96;game_id&#96;
-  - REST endpoints for history; Socket events for new events
+I want to make sure we clarify that the "near jail triple ones" rule is overridden by the jail scenario. I’ll confirm this detail to ensure everything aligns correctly. Afterward, I’ll summarize the key points in concise bullet points for clarity and ease of understanding. This way, it will be straightforward for anyone to follow the rules and understand how they interact with each other. Let’s make it clear and simple!&lt;/details&gt;&lt;/think&gt;
 
-### Persistence (Save/Load)
-- MVP: &#96;redux-persist&#96; + &#96;localForage&#96; to store multiple games and restore by &#96;game_id&#96;
-- Cloud: move event log to DB; local keeps a rolling cache for offline-first (optional later)
+---
 
-### Testing
-- Unit test pure rules (dice, movement, bus, +1/-1) with Vitest
-- Selector tests for derived state (passed GO, pot payouts)
-- Component tests for setup form and turn controls
+### Clarifying “third doubles → jail” with triples involved
 
-### Project structure
-- &#96;src/&#96;
-  - &#96;app/&#96; entry, routing, providers (Redux, PersistGate, Tailwind)
-  - &#96;features/&#96;
-    - &#96;gameEvents/&#96; (slice, selectors, RTK Query endpoints)
-    - &#96;players/&#96; (forms and selectors)
-    - &#96;ui/&#96;
-  - &#96;components/&#96; shared UI (dice input, timeline, pots, modals)
-  - &#96;lib/&#96; rules engine (pure functions), ids, zod schemas
-  - &#96;types/&#96; shared types (align with &#96;src/types/monopoly-schema.ts&#96;)
-  - &#96;styles/&#96; Tailwind setup
-  - &#96;pages/&#96; or route-based components (Vite + React Router)
+- Tracking within a single turn:
+  - Start with doublesStreak = 0.
+  - Each roll:
+    - If doublesStreak == 2 and the current roll is a double OR a triple → go straight to Jail. No teleport, no +$1000, no +$200.
+    - Else if the current roll is a triple:
+      - Handle as triple (teleport; and +$1000 if 1-1-1, except the special “3-steps-to-Jail” case).
+      - Turn ends immediately. Triples never grant another roll.
+      - Because the turn ends, you cannot get “consecutive triples.”
+    - Else if the current roll is a double:
+      - doublesStreak += 1 and roll again.
+    - Else:
+      - Turn ends and doublesStreak resets to 0.
 
-### Initial milestone order
-1) Scaffolding + Tailwind 4 + Redux Toolkit
-2) Data types and event model + selectors
-3) Game setup form (zod, react-hook-form)
-4) Turn workflow: dice → movement → actions (+ undo)
-5) Timeline summary + Save/Load (persist)
-6) Optional: RTK Query wiring stub for later multiplayer
+- Precedence reminder:
+  - If you’ve already rolled two doubles in the same turn, any third roll that is a double or a triple sends you to Jail (overrides triple 1-1-1 bonuses/teleport and “3-steps-to-Jail” variants).
+  - This matches your rule: after two doubles, a triple on the third roll “counts as the third double” → Jail.
 
-### Clarifying questions
-- Do you want “Multiplayer sync” in MVP, or add it after the single-device MVP is done? [[memory:431802]]
-- Preference for backend: Supabase (fastest real-time) or self-hosted Node + Postgres?
-- Are offline-first and cross-device resume important now, or later with auth?

-I pulled current Supabase pricing and quotas, then drafted concise tradeoffs, timelines, and definitions. Answering your four points and confirming Tailwind v4.1.11 now.
-
-### Multiplayer in MVP: pros, cons, time/difficulty
-
-- **Pros**
-  - **Real user value**: remote play, spectators later.
-  - **Architecture early**: data model, IDs, idempotency, conflict resolution, and undo semantics get designed correctly from the start.
-  - **Fewer surprises later**: networking and consistency bugs surface early.
+_**Agent (model gpt-5, mode Agent)**_
 
-- **Cons**
-  - **Complexity**: events must be authoritative, ordered, idempotent, and conflict-safe; undo becomes global, not just local.
-  - **Scope creep**: auth, presence, retries/backoff, partial failures, migrations.
-  - **Slower MVP**: more moving parts to stabilize and test (network, DB, listeners, cache, offline edge cases).
+&lt;think&gt;&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Thought Process&lt;/summary&gt;
+**Planning property game features**
 
-- **Difficulty/time added (rough)**
-  - If single-device MVP is 2–4 dev-days, adding basic realtime multiplayer typically adds +2–5 dev-days (schema, API, subscriptions, optimistic updates, conflict handling, testing).
-  - Production-grade multiplayer (auth, presence, reconnect/queue, rate limits, e2e tests) can add +1–2 weeks.
-  - Rule of thumb: +30–60% effort for “basic realtime”; +2–3× for “production-grade”.
-
-Recommendation: Ship single-device MVP first; add realtime next. It front-loads learning without stalling UI/flows.

-### Supabase vs self-hosted (pros/cons + price)
+The status update spec requests a brief progress note before tool calls, but I won't use any tools in this case. I should include a summary at the end! So, I’m considering adding milestones between stages 1 and 2, focusing on Board &amp; Data, Cards, and Property Improvements. I need to outline data structures for board tiles, card decks, and rules for rent calculations. Additionally, I should clarify the separation of "Bus" cards and think about enforcing building order rules. Let’s piece this together!
+**Exploring the Bus deck concept**
 
-- **Supabase (recommended for speed)**
-  - **Pros**
-    - Managed Postgres, Auth, Realtime, Storage; fast to wire.
-    - Generous free tier; simple Pro plan.
-    - Built-in realtime quotas and metrics.
-  - **Cons**
-    - Vendor lock-in to Postgres + Supabase features.
-    - Free projects pause on inactivity; region options limited.
-    - Overages are usage-based; need monitoring.
-  - **Price snapshot**
-    - Free: 500 MB DB, 1 GB storage, 5 GB egress, 50k MAU, 500k Edge Function calls, 2M realtime messages, 200 peak connections. Sources: [Realtime pricing](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/realtime/pricing), [Edge Functions pricing](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/functions/pricing), [Billing overview](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/platform/billing-on-supabase).
-    - Pro: $25/mo base, includes 8 GB DB, 100 GB storage, 250 GB egress, 100k MAU, 2M Edge Function calls, 5M realtime messages, 500 connections; overages per doc (e.g., $2.50 per 1M realtime messages, $10 per 1000 peak connections). Spend cap available on Pro. See [Billing](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/platform/billing-on-supabase).
+The user brings up the Bus as both a special die face and a card type, which makes me think there could be a Bus deck involved. I'm recalling that in Monopoly Mega Edition, there are Bus Tickets cards that are part of the Chance. I need to decide whether the Bus cards should be their own deck or integrated with Chance. 
 
-- **Self-hosted (Node + Postgres + Socket.IO)**
-  - **Pros**
-    - Full control (infra, scaling, regions).
-    - Potentially lower variable cost at scale or if reusing existing infra.
-    - No vendor-specific constraints; swap components freely.
-  - **Cons**
-    - Ops burden: backups, security, uptime, migrations, observability.
-    - You must build auth, presence, and rate-limiting yourself.
-    - Longer setup time; more code to maintain.
-  - **Price snapshot**
-    - Typical baseline $10–$40/mo for small Postgres + app server on Render/Fly/Railway, plus your time to run/secure it. Scales with usage.
+I'll organize this into milestones 1a-1e and provide a time estimate while confirming the use of Tailwind 4.1.11 and Redux. I’ll keep my questions minimal—just two or three. Let’s move forward!&lt;/details&gt;&lt;/think&gt;
 
-If you want multiplayer soon with minimal friction, Supabase is the pragmatic choice. If you want full control or plan for non-Postgres engines, self-host later.
+---
 
-### Offline-first vs cross-device resume
+### Added milestones for board, cards, and buildings
 
-- **Offline-first**
-  - App works with no network (turns, purchases, undo) using local storage (IndexedDB).
-  - On reconnect, pending events are synced to server; conflicts resolved by server authority and timestamps/versions.
-  - For single-device play, it’s easy and gives resilience. For multiplayer, requires a local queue and idempotent server events.
+- Milestone 1a: Board data and types
+  - Define full board for Mega Monopoly (40+ tiles): &#96;src/data/board.ts&#96;.
+  - Tile metadata: &#96;id&#96;, &#96;index&#96;, &#96;name&#96;, &#96;type&#96;, &#96;group&#96;, &#96;price&#96;, &#96;houseCost&#96;, &#96;hotelCost&#96;, &#96;skyscraperCost&#96;, &#96;mortgageValue&#96;, &#96;rentSchedule&#96; (0–4 houses, hotel, skyscraper).
+  - Selectors: tile lookup by index/id; forward distance; GO index; Jail index.
 
-- **Cross-device resume**
-  - Pick up the same game on another device via cloud state (events stored in DB).
-  - Requires auth or a shareable game token; server is source of truth. Devices subscribe to updates and time-travel on join.
+- Milestone 1b: Cards (Chance, Community Chest, Bus)
+  - Define card decks: &#96;src/data/cards.ts&#96;: arrays for &#96;chance&#96;, &#96;community&#96;, &#96;busTickets&#96; (if separate).
+  - Card type: &#96;id&#96;, &#96;deck&#96;, &#96;text&#96;, &#96;effect&#96; (typed payload: moveTo, pay, receive, goToJail, getOutOfJail, busTicket, etc.).
+  - Shuffling/draw/discard mechanics with deterministic seeds (for reproducible replays).
+  - Events: &#96;CARD_DRAWN&#96;, &#96;CARD_RESOLVED&#96;.
 
-- **For this app**
-  - MVP single-device: use &#96;redux-persist&#96; + IndexedDB; export/import or simple cloud save.
-  - Multiplayer: server event log is canonical; clients apply realtime updates. Offline clients queue events and reconcile on reconnect.
+- Milestone 1c: Property improvements (houses/hotel/skyscraper)
+  - Extend rules engine for build/sell logic:
+    - Purchase flow: &#96;BUY_HOUSE&#96;, &#96;SELL_HOUSE&#96;, &#96;BUY_HOTEL&#96;, &#96;SELL_HOTEL&#96;, &#96;BUY_SKYSCRAPER&#96;, &#96;SELL_SKYSCRAPER&#96;, &#96;MORTGAGE&#96;, &#96;UNMORTGAGE&#96;.
+    - Rent calculation uses current improvement level and monopoly status.
+    - Even-building rule toggle in settings (on/off).
+  - UI: property sheet panel with counts and actions; validation errors surfaced.
 
-### Turn flow confirmation
+- Milestone 2b: Visual board (basic)
+  - Tailwind grid board with tokens, ownership markers, improvement pips.
+  - Click a tile to view details, actions, and history for that tile.
 
-- **Flow**: Action1 (buy/sell/mortgage) → dice → movement → Action2 (pay rent, taxes, etc.) → undo support.
-- **Model**: event pipeline with validation gates:
-  - &#96;PRE_MOVE_ACTION&#96; → &#96;ROLL&#96; → &#96;MOVE&#96; → &#96;POST_MOVE_ACTION&#96; (rent/tax/card) → optional &#96;REVERT_TO(eventId)&#96; for undo.
-- We’ll adopt this order and encode it in the rules engine and UI steps.
+- Milestone 3b: Card UIs
+  - Draw/resolve modals; Bus ticket UI if separate deck.
+  - Timeline entries for card effects.
 
-### Tailwind version
+- Milestone 4b: Stats overlay (per game)
+  - Live aggregates needed for spectators: ownership, rent earned, other income, laps, steps, most-landed tile, etc.

+- Event-first approach keeps undo and spectator views consistent.

-### Direction confirmed

-### Architecture to match this
-- Backend: Supabase (Postgres + Realtime + Auth anon).
-- Data model (core):
-  - &#96;games&#96;: id, owner_uid, share_code, title, created_at, ended_at, winner_player_id, is_public.
-  - &#96;game_players&#96;: id, game_id, name, color, seat_order, starting_money.
-  - &#96;game_events&#96;: id, game_id, seq, actor_player_id, type, payload (jsonb), money_delta, created_at, client_id, client_event_id, reverted_by_event_id.
-- Access:
-  - Host device signs in anonymously → becomes &#96;owner_uid&#96;.
-  - RLS: read by &#96;share_code&#96; (public), write only by &#96;owner_uid&#96;.
-- Realtime:
-  - Spectators subscribe to &#96;game_events&#96; filtered by &#96;game_id&#96; for live stats.
-
-### Stats you listed → how we’ll compute
-- Winner: from &#96;DECLARE_WINNER&#96; event and/or &#96;games.winner_player_id&#96;.
-- Properties owned by player: fold &#96;PURCHASE&#96;, &#96;MORTGAGE&#96;, &#96;UNMORTGAGE&#96;, &#96;TRADE&#96; events into current ownership.
-- Money paid out by player: sum negative &#96;money_delta&#96; grouped by &#96;actor_player_id&#96;.
-- Rent earned by player: sum positive &#96;money_delta&#96; on &#96;RENT_RECEIVED&#96; grouped by owner.
-- Other income (GO, Free Parking, cards): sum positive &#96;money_delta&#96; for types &#96;PASS_GO&#96;, &#96;FREE_PARKING_WIN&#96;, &#96;CARD_REWARD&#96;.
-- Total income by player: sum all positive &#96;money_delta&#96;.
-- Laps around board: count &#96;PASSED_GO&#96; events per player.
-- Steps taken: sum &#96;MOVE.steps&#96; including modifiers (+1/-1/Bus decision).
-- Most landed on property: count &#96;LANDED_ON_TILE(tile_id)&#96;; take max.
-
-MVP implementation note: compute on the client from the event log; later, add SQL views/materialized views for faster spectator loads.
-
-### Share link behavior
-- Read-only link: &#96;/g/:share_code&#96; (anyone can spectate, live updates via Realtime).
-- Host link: same URL, but write allowed because the host’s session uid matches &#96;owner_uid&#96; via anon auth.
-- Optional later: “promote” a spectator to co-host by storing their &#96;uid&#96; in &#96;games.editors&#96;.
-
-### Undo with spectators
-- Host emits &#96;REVERT_TO(event_id)&#96; which appends a new event and marks a range as reverted; spectators receive it via Realtime and recompute.
-
-### Effort and sequencing
-- Cloud save + share link + live spectator stats: ~1–2 dev-days after single-device MVP (schema, anon auth, RLS, basic Realtime wiring, selectors).
-- Production hardening (presence, reconnect queue, idempotency across devices): add ~2–4 days later.
-
-### Tailwind
-- We’ll pin Tailwind v4.1.11.
-
-### Minimal clarifications
-- Is a public read-only share OK, or should we support “private share” (unguessable code plus toggle to disable public listing)?
-- Any PII in player names to consider, or we’re fine with public names in shared games?
-
-- Locked scope to “cloud save + share link” with Supabase anon auth and RLS.
-- Mapped every requested stat to concrete event-derived computations and live spectator updates.
-- Confirmed Tailwind v4.1.11; asked two brief privacy clarifications before scaffolding.
+I’m going to scaffold the project with Vite (React + TS), initialize Git, and set up Tailwind v4.1.11, Redux Toolkit, and React Router. I’ll also verify Tailwind v4.1.11 setup steps to ensure the correct configuration. Then I’ll run the install and first build.
 
 ---

-- what is PII

I faced it too—docs didn’t help much, and outdated commands like &#96;tailwindcss init&#96;had me completely lost.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;So here’s the step-by-step setup that finally worked for me:&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;✅ **Tailwind v4 Setup with React + Vite**&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;1. Create your Vite + React project:  &lt;br/&gt;&#96;npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react&#96;  &lt;br/&gt;&#96;cd my-app&#96;&lt;br/&gt;2. Install Tailwind CSS v4 with Vite plugin:  &lt;br/&gt;&#96;npm install -D tailwindcss@latest @tailwindcss/vite&#96;&lt;br/&gt;3. Create and edit src/index.css:  &lt;br/&gt;&#96;@import "tailwindcss";&#96;&lt;br/&gt;4. Update vite.config.js:&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;import \{ defineConfig \} from 'vite'&lt;br/&gt;import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'&lt;br/&gt;import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;export default defineConfig(\{&lt;br/&gt;  plugins: [react(), tailwindcss()],&lt;br/&gt;\});&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;1. Make sure main.jsx &#96;imports ./index.css&#96;&lt;br/&gt;2. Test it in App.jsx:&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;function App() \{&lt;br/&gt;  return (&lt;br/&gt;    &lt;h1 className="text-4xl font-bold text-blue-600"&gt;&lt;br/&gt;      Hello Tailwind v4!&lt;br/&gt;    &lt;/h1&gt;&lt;br/&gt;  );&lt;br/&gt;\}&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;1. Run your dev server:&#96;npm run dev&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;✅ And you’re all set with Tailwind v4 running in your React app using Vite!&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;😅 Want to Hear the Chaos Behind This?  &lt;br/&gt;If you’re curious about how I actually figured this out—confusion, outdated guides, broken commands, Discord help, and even ChatGPT misfires—I wrote a separate blog post on my full struggle story in Medium.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;👉 Read: 💻 My Tailwind CSS v4 Setup Struggle&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Hope it helps another beginner avoid the same mess I went through! 💙&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;##  Top comments (1) &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Subscribe &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;pic &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Personal Trusted User &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt; Create template &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Templates let you quickly answer FAQs or store snippets for re-use.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Submit Preview Dismiss &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Collapse Expand &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;nayan_anand profile image &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Nayan Anand &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt; Nayan Anand &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt; Nayan Anand &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Follow &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt; Becoming a better developer everyday \| PostgreSQL \| Express \| React \| Node&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* Location  &lt;br/&gt; Chennai, India&lt;br/&gt;* Education  &lt;br/&gt; NSS College of Engineering&lt;br/&gt;* Work  &lt;br/&gt; Freelance Developer&lt;br/&gt;* Joined  &lt;br/&gt;Dec 11, 2024&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;•  Apr 30 &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Dropdown menu &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* Copy link&lt;br/&gt;* Hide&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Thank You so much Lord Potato. scroll-margin&lt;br/&gt;* scroll-padding&lt;br/&gt;* scroll-snap-align&lt;br/&gt;* scroll-snap-stop&lt;br/&gt;* scroll-snap-type&lt;br/&gt;* touch-action&lt;br/&gt;* user-select&lt;br/&gt;* will-change&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;### SVG&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* fill&lt;br/&gt;* stroke&lt;br/&gt;* stroke-width&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;### Accessibility&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* forced-color-adjust&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Installation&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;# Get started with Tailwind CSS&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Tailwind CSS works by scanning all of your HTML files, JavaScript components, and any other templates for class names, generating the corresponding styles and then writing them to a static CSS file.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;It's fast, flexible, and reliable — with zero-runtime.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;## Installation&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* ## Using Vite&lt;br/&gt;* ## Using PostCSS&lt;br/&gt;* ## Tailwind CLI&lt;br/&gt;* ## Framework Guides&lt;br/&gt;* ## Play CDN&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;### Installing Tailwind CSS as a Vite plugin&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Installing Tailwind CSS as a Vite plugin is the most seamless way to integrate it with frameworks like Laravel, SvelteKit, React Router, Nuxt, and SolidJS.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;01&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#### Create your project&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Start by creating a new Vite project if you don’t have one set up already. The most common approach is to use Create Vite.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Terminal&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;npm create vite@latest my-projectcd my-project&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;02&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#### Install Tailwind CSS&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Install &#96;tailwindcss&#96; and &#96;@tailwindcss/vite&#96; via npm.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Terminal&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;03&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#### Configure the Vite plugin&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Add the &#96;@tailwindcss/vite&#96; plugin to your Vite configuration.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;vite.config.ts&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;import \{ defineConfig \} from 'vite'import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'export default defineConfig(\{  plugins: [    tailwindcss(),  ],\})&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;04&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#### Import Tailwind CSS&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Add an &#96;@import&#96; to your CSS file that imports Tailwind CSS.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;CSS&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;@import "tailwindcss";&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;05&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#### Start your build process&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Run your build process with &#96;npm run dev&#96; or whatever command is configured in your &#96;package.json&#96; file.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Terminal&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;npm run dev&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;06&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#### Start using Tailwind in your HTML&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Make sure your compiled CSS is included in the &#96;&lt;head&gt;&#96; _(your framework might handle this for you)_, then start using Tailwind’s utility classes to style your content.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;HTML&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;!doctype html&gt;&lt;html&gt;&lt;head&gt;  &lt;meta charset="UTF-8"&gt;  &lt;meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"&gt;  &lt;link href="/src/style.css" rel="stylesheet"&gt;&lt;/head&gt;&lt;body&gt;  &lt;h1 class="text-3xl font-bold underline"&gt;    Hello world!  &lt;/h1&gt;&lt;/body&gt;&lt;/html&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;**Are you stuck?** Setting up Tailwind with Vite can be a bit different across different build tools. Check our framework guides to see if we have more specific instructions for your particular setup.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Explore our framework guides&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;### Tailwind CSS&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* Documentation&lt;br/&gt;* Playground&lt;br/&gt;* Blog&lt;br/&gt;* Showcase&lt;br/&gt;* Sponsor&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;### Resources&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* Refactoring UI&lt;br/&gt;* Headless UI&lt;br/&gt;* Heroicons&lt;br/&gt;* Hero Patterns&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;### Tailwind Plus&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* UI Blocks&lt;br/&gt;* Templates&lt;br/&gt;* UI Kit&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;### Community&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* GitHub&lt;br/&gt;* Discord&lt;br/&gt;* X&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;### Tailwind CSS&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* Documentation&lt;br/&gt;* Playground&lt;br/&gt;* Blog&lt;br/&gt;* Showcase&lt;br/&gt;* Sponsor&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;### Tailwind Plus&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* UI Blocks&lt;br/&gt;* Templates&lt;br/&gt;* UI Kit&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;### Resources&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* Refactoring UI&lt;br/&gt;* Headless UI&lt;br/&gt;* Heroicons&lt;br/&gt;* Hero Patterns&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;### Community&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* GitHub&lt;br/&gt;* Discord&lt;br/&gt;* X&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Copyright © 2025 Tailwind Labs Inc.·Trademark Policy |

Report Abuse &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Cover image for How to setup Tailwind CSS v4.1.5 with Vite + React (2025 updated guide) &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Imamul Islam Ifti &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Imamul Islam Ifti &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt; Posted on May 7 &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#  How to setup Tailwind CSS v4.1.5 with Vite + React (2025 updated guide)&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#webdev #react #tailwindcss #vite &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Tailwind CSS v4+ has a new setup method when using Vite and React. This post walks you through the latest official installation process as outlined on the Tailwind CSS docs.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;##  1\. Prerequisites&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Make sure you have Node.js (v18+) and npm installed:  &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;node -v&lt;br/&gt;npm -v&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;##  2\. Create a new Vite + React project&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;npm create vite@latest my-portfolio -- --template react&lt;br/&gt;cd my-portfolio&lt;br/&gt;npm install&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;##  3\. Install Tailwind CSS&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Install tailwindcss and @tailwindcss/vite via npm.  &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;##  4\. Configure the Vite plugin&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Add the @tailwindcss/vite plugin to your Vite configuration file(vite.config.js).  &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;import \{ defineConfig \} from 'vite'&lt;br/&gt;import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'&lt;br/&gt;export default defineConfig(\{&lt;br/&gt;  plugins: [&lt;br/&gt;    react(), tailwindcss(),&lt;br/&gt;  ],&lt;br/&gt;\})&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;##  5\. Import Tailwind CSS&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Add an @import to your CSS file (index.css) that imports Tailwind CSS.  &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;@import "tailwindcss";&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;##  6\. Test&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;In your app.jsx file replace the code with  &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;div className="min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center bg-black text-white text-4xl font-bold"&gt;&lt;br/&gt;      Tailwind is working!&lt;br/&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;##  7\. Start your build process&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Run your build process with npm run dev or whatever command is configured in your package.json file.  &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;npm run dev&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Was this helpful? Let me know in the comments and follow for more!&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;##  Top comments (0) &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Subscribe &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;pic &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Personal Trusted User &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt; Create template &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Templates let you quickly answer FAQs or store snippets for re-use.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Submit Preview Dismiss &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Code of Conduct • Report abuse &lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt; Are you sure you want to hide this comment? forced-color-adjust&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Installation&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;# Get started with Tailwind CSS&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Tailwind CSS works by scanning all of your HTML files, JavaScript components, and any other templates for class names, generating the corresponding styles and then writing them to a static CSS file.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;It's fast, flexible, and reliable — with zero-runtime.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;## Installation&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;* ## Using Vite&lt;br/&gt;* ## Using PostCSS&lt;br/&gt;* ## Tailwind CLI&lt;br/&gt;* ## Framework Guides&lt;br/&gt;* ## Play CDN&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;### Installing Tailwind CSS as a Vite plugin&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Installing Tailwind CSS as a Vite plugin is the most seamless way to integrate it with frameworks like Laravel, SvelteKit, React Router, Nuxt, and SolidJS.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;01&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#### Create your project&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Start by creating a new Vite project if you don’t have one set up already. The most common approach is to use Create Vite.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Terminal&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;npm create vite@latest my-projectcd my-project&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;02&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#### Install Tailwind CSS&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Install &#96;tailwindcss&#96; and &#96;@tailwindcss/vite&#96; via npm.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Terminal&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;03&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#### Configure the Vite plugin&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Add the &#96;@tailwindcss/vite&#96; plugin to your Vite configuration.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;vite.config.ts&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;import \{ defineConfig \} from 'vite'import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'export default defineConfig(\{  plugins: [    tailwindcss(),  ],\})&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;04&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#### Import Tailwind CSS&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Add an &#96;@import&#96; to your CSS file that imports Tailwind CSS.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;CSS&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;@import "tailwindcss";&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;05&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#### Start your build process&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Run your build process with &#96;npm run dev&#96; or whatever command is configured in your &#96;package.json&#96; file.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Terminal&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;npm run dev&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;06&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;#### Start using Tailwind in your HTML&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;Make sure your compiled CSS is included in the &#96;&lt;head&gt;&#96; _(your framework might handle this for you)_, then start using Tailwind’s utility classes to style your content.&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;HTML&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;!doctype html&gt;&lt;html&gt;&lt;head&gt;  &lt;meta charset="UTF-8"&gt;  &lt;meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"&gt;  &lt;link href="/src/style.css" rel="stylesheet"&gt;&lt;/head&gt;&lt;body&gt;  &lt;h1 class="text-3xl font-bold underline"&gt;    Hello world!  &lt;/h1&gt;&lt;/body&gt;&lt;/html&gt;&lt;br/&gt;&#96;&#96;&#96;&lt;br/&gt;&lt;br/&gt;**Are you stuck?** Setting up Tailwind with Vite can be a bit different across different build tools. -### Rules confirmed
-- No extra turn on any triples.
-- Triple 1s: +$1000 and “move anywhere.”

+&lt;think&gt;&lt;details&gt;&lt;summary&gt;Thought Process&lt;/summary&gt;
+**Evaluating Node and Vite Compatibility**
 
-### Individual/lifetime stats scope
-- Per-game stats (MVP): easy, computed from the event log; supports live spectator view.
-- Lifetime stats across games: adds scope, needs a stable player identity.
-  - Identity options:
-    - Anonymous, device-bound &#96;player_profile_id&#96; (stored locally; can be “claimed” later if you add auth).
-    - Nickname-only is unreliable for aggregation.
-  - Storage/compute options:
-    - On-demand SQL views (low complexity, may be slower on many games).
-    - Denormalized &#96;player_aggregate_stats&#96; table updated by triggers or a scheduled job (faster reads, more setup).
-  - Effort:
-    - Device-bound profiles + lifetime aggregation (views first): ~0.5–1 day.
-    - Denormalized aggregates with triggers/scheduled job: +0.5–1.5 days more.
-
-### Stat coverage plan

-### Short answer
-Yes. We can ship MVP without identities and add a device-bound &#96;player_profile_id&#96; later to enable individual/lifetime stats.
-
-### How we’ll do it later (no MVP rework)
-- Add tables/columns (backward-compatible):
-  - &#96;player_profiles&#96;: &#96;id&#96;, &#96;device_hash&#96;, &#96;created_at&#96;.
-  - &#96;game_players.profile_id&#96; (nullable).
-- Client generates/keeps a &#96;profile_id&#96; in local storage for the device.
-- When creating players, attach &#96;profile_id&#96; to that player.
-- Lifetime stats = SQL views over &#96;game_events&#96; grouped by &#96;profile_id&#96;:
-  - wins, total steps, laps, triples rolled, free parking wins, most purchased property, best single-game earnings.
-- Past games remain valid; they just have &#96;profile_id = null&#96; until linked.
-
-Time: ~0.5 day to add identities + basic lifetime views; ~1 day if we also add UI for a “My lifetime stats” page.
-
-Note: Device-bound means aggregation only on the same device. If you want cross-device later, we can add an optional “claim profile” (magic link) to unify profiles.
-
-Open rule detail still needed:
-- For non-111 teleports that wrap past GO, should we also award +$200, or only increment laps?

-### Rule locked: third doubles → jail overrides everything
-- On a player’s third consecutive double, they go straight to jail. No movement, no teleport, no +$200, no +$1000 even if the third roll is 1-1-1.
-- Precedence in the rules engine:
-  1) If third consecutive double: emit JAIL {reason:'THREE_DOUBLES'} and end turn.
-  2) Else if triple 1-1-1: award +$1000, then TELEPORT (steps=0; if wrap GO, +$200).
-  3) Else if other triples: TELEPORT (steps=0; if wrap GO, +$200).
-  4) Else: normal MOVE with +1/-1/Bus logic.

++ import { createSlice, PayloadAction } from '@reduxjs/toolkit';
++ import type { GameEvent } from '@/types/monopoly-schema';

++ type EventsState = {
++   events: GameEvent[];

++ 
++ const initialState: EventsState = {
++   events: [],

++ 
++ const eventsSlice = createSlice({
++   name: 'events',
++   initialState,
++   reducers: {
++     appendEvent(state, action: PayloadAction&amp;lt;GameEvent&amp;gt;) {

++     resetEvents(state) {
++       state.events = [];
++     },
++   },
++ });
++ 
++ export const { appendEvent, resetEvents } = eventsSlice.actions;
++ export default eventsSlice.reducer;

-### Rule locked
-- Third consecutive doubles OR two doubles then any triples → go straight to Jail. No teleport, no +$1000, no +$200 for GO.
-- Triple ones (1-1-1):
-  - If the player is 3 steps away from the Jail tile (forward distance): go straight to Jail. No teleport, no +$1000, no GO payout.
-  - Otherwise: +$1000 and Teleport (steps=0). If the chosen teleport wraps past GO, award +$200.
-- Other triples (2-2-2 or 3-3-3): Teleport only (steps=0). If the chosen teleport wraps past GO, award +$200.
+“Apple Software” means the Xcode Developer Tools and the Apple SDKs, including any Updates thereto (if any) 
+that may be provided to You by Apple.
 
-### Engine precedence (per roll)
-1) If third double or (two doubles then triple): JAIL {reason:'THREE_DOUBLES'|'DOUBLE_DOUBLE_TRIPLE'}.
-2) Else if 1-1-1 and distToJailForward === 3: JAIL {reason:'TRIPLE_ONES_NEAR_JAIL'}.
-3) Else if 1-1-1: JACKPOT_111 (+1000) → TELEPORT (steps=0) → optional PASSED_GO (+200).
-4) Else if other triple: TELEPORT (steps=0) → optional PASSED_GO (+200).
-5) Else: normal MOVE (+1/-1/Bus resolution).

-- Tracking within a single turn:
-  - Start with doublesStreak = 0.
-  - Each roll:
-    - If doublesStreak == 2 and the current roll is a double OR a triple → go straight to Jail. No teleport, no +$1000, no +$200.
-    - Else if the current roll is a triple:
-      - Handle as triple (teleport; and +$1000 if 1-1-1, except the special “3-steps-to-Jail” case).
-      - Turn ends immediately. Triples never grant another roll.
-      - Because the turn ends, you cannot get “consecutive triples.”
-    - Else if the current roll is a double:
-      - doublesStreak += 1 and roll again.
-    - Else:
-      - Turn ends and doublesStreak resets to 0.

-&#96;&#96;&#96;
+**Option A: Using &#96;nvm&#96;**  
+- Install &#96;nvm&#96;: &#96;curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.7/install.sh | bash&#96;.
+- Source &#96;nvm&#96;: &#96;export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm"; [ -s "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" ] &amp;&amp; . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"&#96;.
+- Install and use Node 22 LTS: &#96;nvm install 22 &amp;&amp; nvm alias default 22 &amp;&amp; nvm use 22&#96;.
+- Verify: &#96;node -v&#96;.
 
-&lt;/details&gt;
+**Option B: Using Homebrew**  
+- Run: &#96;brew install node@22&#96;.
+- Update your path: &#96;echo 'export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin:$PATH"' &gt;&gt; /.zshrc &amp;&amp; source /.zshrc&#96;.
+
+After upgrading Node, run &#96;npm run build&#96; again. Finally, initialize Git with the following commands:
+
+- &#96;git init&#96;
+- &#96;git add -A&#96;
+- &#96;git commit -m "chore: initial scaffold"&#96;.
+
+Please let me know which Node upgrade method you prefer. After completing these steps, we can track our progress with milestones!&lt;/details&gt;&lt;/think&gt;

+### Accept the Xcode license (required for Git on macOS)
+1) Accept license (non-interactive):
+- &#96;sudo xcodebuild -license accept&#96;
+2) If you see “no developer tools were found”:
+- &#96;xcode-select --install&#96; (accept the GUI prompt), then rerun:
+- &#96;sudo xcodebuild -license accept&#96;
+3) Verify Git works:
+- &#96;git --version&#96;

-&#96;&#96;&#96;
+Option A — Recommended (nvm; easy to switch versions)
+1) Install nvm:
+- &#96;curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.7/install.sh | bash&#96;
+- &#96;export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm"; [ -s "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" ] &amp;&amp; . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"&#96;
+2) Install and use Node 22 LTS:
+- &#96;nvm install 22&#96;
+- &#96;nvm alias default 22&#96;
+- &#96;nvm use 22&#96;
+3) Verify:
+- &#96;node -v&#96;
 
-&lt;/details&gt;
+Option B — Homebrew (system-wide)
+- Apple Silicon:
+  - &#96;brew install node@22&#96;
+  - &#96;echo 'export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin:$PATH"' &gt;&gt; ~/.zshrc &amp;&amp; source ~/.zshrc&#96;
+- Intel:
+  - &#96;brew install node@22&#96;
+  - &#96;echo 'export PATH="/usr/local/opt/node@22/bin:$PATH"' &gt;&gt; ~/.zshrc &amp;&amp; source ~/.zshrc&#96;
+- Verify: &#96;node -v&#96;
+
+### After that
+- I’ll run: &#96;git init &amp;&amp; git add -A &amp;&amp; git commit -m "chore: initial scaffold"&#96; and proceed with Milestone 1a (&#96;src/data/board.ts&#96; with full board metadata + helpers).
+
+Quick pick: prefer nvm (Recommended) or Homebrew (Easy system-wide)?

-ot limited to header files, APIs, libraries, simulators, and software (source code and object code) labeled
-as part of the macOS SDK and included in the Xcode Developer Tools package for purposes of targeting Apple-b
-randed products running macOS.

- limited to, imagery, terrain data, latitude and longitude coordinates, transit data, points of interest and
- traffic data.
-
-“Program Requirements” mean the technical, human interface, design, product category, security, performance,
- and other criteria and requirements for Applications specified by Apple in the Apple Developer Program Lice
-nse Agreement.
-
-“Provisioning Profiles” means the time-limited files (including applicable entitlements, certificates or oth
-er identifiers) that may be provided by Apple for use by You in connection with Your Application development
- and testing.
-
-“Updates” means bug fixes, updates, upgrades, modifications, enhancements and supplements to the Apple Softw
-are or Apple Services, and any new releases or versions thereof.
-
-“Xcode Developer Tools” means the Apple-proprietary development platform, including but not limited to softw
-are tools, compilers, sample code, Documentation, programming languages, simulators, and overall design pack
-age provided by Apple hereunder, excluding the Apple SDKs.
-
-“You” or “Your” means the person(s) or entity using the Apple Software or Services or otherwise exercising r
-ights under this Agreement.  If You are entering into this Agreement on behalf of Your company, organization
-, educational institution, or an agency, instrumentality, or department of the federal government, “You” or

-ow automatic updates for the Apple Software in System Preferences, then Your Apple-branded computer will per

-ailable, the update or upgrade will automatically download and install onto Your Apple-branded computer and,
- if applicable, Your peripheral devices. You can turn off the automatic updates for the Apple Software at an
-y time by changing the automatic updates settings within System Preferences.  Apple is not obligated to prov
-ide any maintenance, technical or other support for the Apple Software and Apple Services.  You acknowledge

-e and/or that Apple makes available to You through other mechanisms, e.g., through the use of keys that Appl
-e may make accessible to You through the Apple Software or Apple Services.  You agree to access such Apple S
-ervices only through the mechanisms provided by Apple for such access and only for use on Apple-branded prod
-ucts.  Except as expressly permitted by Apple in writing, You agree not to share access to mechanisms provid
-ed to You by Apple for the use of Apple Services with any third party.  Further, You agree not to create or
-attempt to create a substitute or similar service through use of or access to the Apple Services.

-You agree to access and use such Services only as necessary for providing services and functionality for You
-r Applications that are eligible to use such Services and only as permitted by Apple in writing, including i
-n the Documentation.  You may not use the Apple Services in any manner that is inconsistent with the terms o

-2.10    Digital Materials, Reality Composer and Reality Composer Pro Software
-A.      The Reality Composer and/or Reality Composer Pro software (“Composer Software”) that may be included
- with the Apple Software may contain, display and provide access to certain content, photographs, text, imag
-es, graphics, artwork, audio, video, augmented reality or similar assets (collectively, the “Digital Materia
-ls”). Title and intellectual property rights in the Digital Materials belong to Apple or the respective cont
-ent owner, and such Digital Materials may be protected by copyright or other intellectual property laws and
-treaties, or subject to terms of use of the third party providing such Digital Materials.  Apple is not obli
-gated to provide for, and does not guarantee, the availability of the Composer Software or any specific Digi
-tal Materials.  Any Updates to the Composer Software may therefore contain some Digital Materials different
-from the previous versions.
-
-B.      Automatic Updates of the Composer Software may also include updates to the library of Digital Materi
-als that are provided as part of the Composer Software. Regardless of whether automatic updates are enabled
-or not, when the Composer Software is in use, the Composer Software will automatically check with Apple for
-Updates to the Digital Materials. If an Update is available, You will be prompted to download the updated or
- new Digital Materials. Apple reserves the right to automatically modify, remove, or delete any Digital Mate
-rials on Your Apple-branded computer from time to time if Apple becomes aware of or has reason to believe th
-at it is necessary to cease the distribution and use of such Digital Materials, due to reasons such as legal
- or regulatory requirements, public morals, or technical considerations.  By using the Apple Software, You u
-nderstand and agree that Apple may automatically modify, remove or delete any Digital Materials.
-
-C.      Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, You may use the Digital Materials, on a royalty-free
- basis, to create your own original content for your game, application, or other audiovisual project (“User
-Content”) subject to Apple’s right to modify, remove or delete specific Digital Materials on Your Apple-bran
-ded computer as set forth in Section 2.10B above. You may display and distribute User Content that was creat
-ed using the Digital Materials as part of or in connection with Your game, application or other audiovisual
-project; however, individual Digital Materials may not be commercially or otherwise distributed on a standal
-one basis, or repackaged in whole or in part as audio clips, visual content, or asset packs.
-
-D.      Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, (i) this Agreement does not grant You any rights to 
-use such Digital Materials, nor does it obligate Apple to make any such Digital Materials available to You c
-ontinually or for any specific term, and (ii) You may not use the Digital Materials outside the context of t
-heir intended use as part of the Apple Software. Without prejudice to Section 2.12C below, You agree not to
-use the Composer Software, including any Digital Materials, for any unlawful or illegal activity or to creat
-e User Content or assets that are offensive or inappropriate. You further agree not to interfere with, disru
-pt, or create an undue burden on servers or networks connected to Your Apple-branded computer or Apple Softw
-are or violate the regulations, policies or procedures of such networks.
-
